title: MadVibes Hostel
address: C. de Mesonero Romanos, Centro, 28013 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Mohamed Hesham (ETSH)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWIUkD4b7RJcMidBKF8LToHOMVusGJ9fizD2Aj_XneYsHR6tas=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"My stay at this hostel was extremely disappointing and far below acceptable standards. From the moment of arrival, the check-in process was not ready on time and lacked basic organization and professionalism.

The overall cleanliness and maintenance were poor. There was an unpleasant smell throughout the property. The beds were uncomfortable, not properly clean, and had no curtains for privacy. The lockers did not lock properly, which raised serious concerns about security.

The bathroom facilities were inadequate — there were no functional locks on the doors, no hooks to hang personal belongings, and no trash bin in the room. These are basic necessities that should be standard in any accommodation.

Additionally, there was no kitchen and not even a microwave available, which is generally considered a basic hostel facility.

During my stay, guests were required to check out and check in again without clear justification, and I was asked to wait outside before being allowed to re-enter. This caused unnecessary inconvenience.

The written policy stated that card payments were not accepted, yet a mandatory €15 deposit was required by card. This inconsistency raises concerns regarding transparency.

It was also very difficult to leave a review on Hostelworld.com , which limits transparency and prevents potential guests from easily seeing honest feedback from others.

Overall, the management, organization, hygiene, and guest handling were below acceptable hospitality standards. Based on my experience, I would not recommend this property","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCQtMD_QqZVnIeuvVm1j0Tc&fid=0x0:0x6e7eba099e8c441a"],"When":"2026-2-14"},{"Name":"Brayan Palace","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ0ZNNUE6S5ZKeCt2988HrV2nzP-uTwSYir0yQzxO_zcf9yOw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"In the evening shift, there was a staff member from Venezuela (I don’t remember his name). title: Multi Hostel Calvario
address: C. del Calvario, 19, Centro, 28012 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Egbert Scherman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU_5wRHDGj_eE5W1UefgbZUu2ONRulAvlxUkcym6tKwqy4vu6IG=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"I had the unfortunate situation where I booked my accommodation 1st before booking my train ticket. When I booked the only train ticket I get find, it meant I would arrive in Madrid at 12 midnight.

Trying to contact the Hostel to make arrangements accordingly proved to be challenging (Since checkin is from 3pm till 11:30pm). After a Google search for a different number, I spoke with a manager/owner who suggested they will wait for me as long as I keep them informed on my arrival in Madrid and as I'm making way to the Hostel. Train arrival was scheduled at 00:00 and it would take 30min to get to the Hostel.

I thought all is in order. An arrangement was made. However- while I was on the train to Madrid I received a call from the Hostel requesting my arrival time. When I responded that I would be there at 00:30 the person on the other end got upset and kept on saying that I can't checkin after 00:00
I received another call, which sounded like a different person, who started fighting with me in Spanish. I kept on saying that I don't understand Spanish.
He then whatsapped me, fighting, and said I have to make sure I'm there before 11:30. I replied that I'm on a train, and obviously can't make the train go faster, and that I have made arrangements at the time of booking with them. He kept on fighting, stating that it's unacceptable.
ALL HIS MESSAGES WAS IN CAPITALS.

I tried to stay calm, and not respond to his messages as I feel the arrangement was made. I informed them on my arrival in Chamartin, and when I got onto the metro.
This unset him more as he started going on (in capitals) that I should have taken a taxi cause the metro takes an hour.
Truth is metro takes 40 min, and a taxi would have taken me 35 min. Metro cost me a few euros, where a taxi would have cost alot. I would expect the person at the hostel should know this, but obviously being very abusive.

When I arrived (at 00:35), the receptionist was friendly, and said his boss is the one who messaged me. And I had to pay €10 for checkin after 11:30 (uhm).

When I made the booking I requested the bottom bed as I'm a left lower leg amputee, and struggle to get up to the top bed. When I remove the metal leg, it's even worse. They replied that it's in order.

When I arrived, guess which bed did I receive? When I queried it, the receptionist seemingly can't do anything about it.

Rigat (I hope the spelling is correct) the receptionist, did come to save the day, and moved me the following day to the bottom bed.

I realized that their wifi isn't working, and they ignored my message of enquiry - twice. Once in the morning and later in the afternoon. When Rigat came to sort my bed, I informed him on the wifi, which he then got sorted as well.

It is obvious that this place will disregard any pre-arrangements or complaint you might have. Treat you like you're an idiot for not falling into their momentarily preferences. 1 Bonus point for Rigat though to safe the day.

Everything else of this place is very much in comparison to other places in the same price range. user_reviews:

The beds are enclosed boxes with 3 walls and a ceiling, and curtains to close the front. This means you can sleep in the dark at any time. Additionally, there's a socket and light inside the box.
There's bathrooms inside the room (in my case 2 toilets and 2 showers for 6 people) which are very clean and modern and they even provide shower gel.
The people at the reception are super nice.

title: LooP Las Ventas Adults Only
address: C. de la Vertiente, 4, Cdad. Lineal, 28017 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Pri Ve","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIUFQNRP791CZ6DyavyMDoYeruUfi8QoAzZg4-RwIy5RwkGaw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Because of the bad reviews I took an extra blanket and towels, this was NOT necessary. The room was warm and even got an extra electric heater, which was not needed. The room gives 2 big clean white towels and a little towel to put on the floor.
The room was clean enough, nothing special just a bed a table and a small chair.
I had no problem with the water, you can make it any temperature you want and also the pressure was really good, the only problem in the shower was that the water was not draining fast enough, so I needed to shower fast to not let it overflow.
The check-in was perfectly easy and better than waiting in the line at a desk.
My biggest problem was indeed as a lot of people already mentioned the noice, it is really as if the other guests are in the same room, we didn't had noisy neighbours they were very quiet but we indeed could hear their conversation, laughing, toilet and shower use.
Also I think all rooms have windows, the cleaning lady was busy cleaning and there were a lot of rooms open and everyone I passed had a window.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Anarely Acevedo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXZpcVpE6LVzMAI4uyV5xXWNbv0oNi2TvwFKXm8DA8pSs4oJNjOLQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I was very skeptical after reading all the bad reviews because we had already booked the reservation. Sleeping , I stayed in 3 different hostel in Madrid and I have the worst night there… you can hear the people from outside and also in the room when they arrived to the room at 3 am .
I know is a share place but even from the share areas the sound was loud.
The beds are confortable and good space.
Probably less people room or private will be best!
Great check in and always someone to help!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alfonso F","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK-MA_NnG1flUMWzvJp_Sm7U2TBfeX6d-wto6dPGNGgE_kodQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Stayed for a couple of weeks in a shared dormitory while moving to Madrid. Had a great stay thanks to Maika and her team which spend an incredible amount of energy in taking care of the guests and looking after the facility. Hostel experiences can depend on other guests you share the room with but what concerns the management of the structure nothing to complain of. The service, cleanliness and availability of the staff result in a fantastic offer. Comfortable beds and plenty of common areas.
Surely recommend","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chloe Man","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUBqZpBe_LTn1AviBCvsNJVmDx54PbNm2e1VzYJ9eMOmjvW-RhU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We booked a private room, which allowed us to have more privacy. I love the decor and the bedding. Bed is comfortable as well.

The ladies at the front desk are wonderful and welcoming. Great amenities. The location is convenient and we didn’t need to call a car at all and were able to walk to every tourist attractions in Madrid.

The only complaint is the noise. We had the room by the street, so we can hear everything that’s happening on the street. Granted the street is relatively quiet, it could still get loud from time to time. The wall is also not soundproof, we could hear everything outside in the hallway (people dragging their luggage / opening lock pad ).","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA29FLSKOpuP92LlTyvSkg1&fid=0x0:0x82550338db99b356"],"When":"2025-12-5"},{"Name":"Kat A.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV-14qq48hckC2M4KTyUmfML2teREuH6DWrY4txKozK4vY6pPAR=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I booked a room for my last night in Madrid while returning from a round trip in Spain. My review is based on this.

Pros:
The hostel is in comfortable walking distance from Atocha, even with luggage, one can walk there in 15 mins max. Also it is close to the centre and sights in the city.
Supermarkets are closeby if you want to buy food, also pubs, small tavernas.
They have a 24-hour reception so no matter what time you arrive, really early, really late, you will be able to check in.
The facilities are nicely designed with good taste, everything was clean. The beds have privacy curtains - this is super!
The shower rooms have enough space and nice and hot water, which is also excellent, there are hair dryers to use.
There are shared spaces for co-working (with conference cabins), dining room, hangout room, fitness room. There is a kitchen area and fridges to use as well. Plenty of rooms everywhere.
The beds are comfortable and has a little light, electric plug and shelve to put your things. Under the bed (extra spacious) storage, hook on the wall for your towel and coat.
Extra luggage lockers are also available on the corridors.
I booked my space in an 8-bed mixed dormitory which was the best value option and added breakfast as well.

Neutral:
This is a cashless hostel, only card payments are accepted.
The bathroom downstairs is cleaned between 8-9am, and it's closed for that time, one has to go to the upper bathroom in that time.
To get towels are €4 - non-refundable.
The doors are operated via GoKey app.

Cons:
The area surrounding the hostel has some rougher parts.
In the morning, I passed a few groups who were drunk or high, loud and generally just loitering.
Inside the hostel, some guests were also interesting.
Without wanting to make the hostel look bad, I just want to draw your attention to this: if you would like a quiet night, book the private room or the "ladies only" room if you are a girl.
In my mixed dorm, the stench of feet was so intense that it woke me up by 3am. Then someone sprayed men's deodorant in the air, which made everything worse. The other guests threw all of their clothes and shoes on the floor, in an untidy manner.
Two people were snoring like hell, someone had their reading light on for the whole night.
Others pointed out also, there are motion sensor activated floor lights in the rooms. These did not bother me, but they do stay on for long. I don't like darkness, so I was fine with it.
In my room, just two things were broken: The underbed storage that belonged to my bed (could not close fully) and the hook that was meant to be mine. No issues, I took another hook and still could place my luggage.
The breakfast did not worth for me personally. It has one coffe, one pastry, a glass of orange juice and one piece of fruit.

While eating breakfast, I witnessed one of the guests harrassing the receptionist girl. It was a disturbing scene, I felt sorry for the girl.

Summary:
Based on my experiences, there are two types of hostels. One type is small, old, maybe a bit outdated too, giving out an alternative/hippie vibe. These attract an amazing community, and have a welcoming, friendly atmosphere despite their less shiny appearance. Don't expect this here.

The other type, like The Loft House are "new concept" hostels, maybe we could describe them as cheap hotel alternatives, with beautiful design, large capacity but they feel alienating.
If you are on a budget, it's still a good choice in a city where accommodation tends to be expensive.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Roos","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVre1aDHQ4kUD8PlBfIPzKfJJQp0DRuLv8a32vPabokVa_iwmY=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"For a schooltrip we booked a whole room at the hostel: I really enjoyed our stay.
The (bath)rooms were super clean, i felt really safe & the receptionist were so kind.
The metrostation and supermarket are nearby & the city centre is walking distance.
Lavapiés is a cool place to stay as well: if you look for it, you can find lovely (tapas) restaurants & bars (definitely not too expensive)","Images":null,"When":""}]

Not only regarding the guests, but essentially for the neighbours. There is not an emergency exit; the smoke alarm seems not to have light, not sure if it works. I was given with a mini-room, too tiny although I asked for a bedroom with bath. The corridors are too tiny. Towels too small, also. As stated, firstly I do not think that it is a secure place; things can happened, emergencies, etc. one old couple got confused with the codes and they could not access the main door to the bedrooms, I just arrived (around 9 pm) and help them. title: HOSTAL ACAPULCO
address: C. de la Salud, 13, 4º, Centro, 28013 Madrid
descriptions: Unpretentious rooms in a modest hotel offering a guest lounge, plus parking & Wi-Fi.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"J. Hong","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJY60uC1RIs3Uof78tcAIoeFdkxw2oLTlU6oG8o_nqJi1LzWA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We stayed five nights plus one more night here. Excellent location. Close to Sol and Gran Via metro stations. Walkable to Prado and royal palace. A good Chinese restaurant across the street, and another good Si Chuan restaurant in 4 minutes.

Room not big, but well designed and functional. Our room was facing inside and was very quiet, except for some noisy neighbor sometimes. Ear plugs helped in those cases.

Friendly staff and owner. Hotel front desk staffed 24 hours. Since we stayed for more than 4 nights, we had a free laundry done by the hotel staff, which was very convenient to us and of great value. Owner also kindly agreed to hold a guidebook of Prado museum for us to pick up when we return at the end of our trip before our flight out.

We would stay here again for our future Madrid trip.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Carmela","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU0fMo9dfgoIdzM2ubLaaQbwM8pLTlataTCj911lIETO6s-ITE_=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Our family of 5 adults loved our stay at Acapulco. Clean, comfortable rooms, kind and welcoming hosts, generous service and excellent location. We felt at ease and at home. Thank you Javier and the rest of the team.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chris Sayers","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIbVrewtxaEoVEHjZcBeOi0hgFRgy2gE3KpzkBFDF5vtECVHQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We've stayed here several times since 2009 and it is always a pleasure. Clean, comfortable and great location. Wonderful staff.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alan Jackson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJjdB5Ay7Iq00UBZsSdltMbyC7etjrn8kNtdFJ06-KRCMrbVQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We spent 5 nights at Hostal Acapulco. The room was clean but small and basic. It reminded me of some convent stays we had done in Italy. The location was excellent, walking distance to many attractions. The area was safe. The front desk was extremely friendly and helpful. Marco, especially, gave us great advice on transportation and dining. The hotel is located on the 3rd floor but the elevator was always available. We would stay here again.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"frank Quimbo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWSZZdcTgYyzyTwr75iV1oD-hFfKHM0QIcnnblMcJi340Nkyw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Hostal Acapulco is conveniently located in the centre of Madrid. The staff were very helpful and friendly,  A comfortable place to sleep and rest while enjoying Madrid.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"barbaralB174OP","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0Qdvd3mFTmnQslJajBtyC7hGXBv5KKEN_7z5ZnXByEfDkAAFDmgEV3HW1P9_iXApXyf1XA=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"I was most fortunate in booking Hostal Acapulco for my first visit to Madrid; the reason being that it’s location on a vast open square halfway between Puerta Del Sol and Gran Via set me up for accessing the city in any direction that I needed to go. Taxis service the adjacent curbside, which meant that I could be dropped off from Barajas airport right at the hostal’s doorstep. Taking the elevator to the fourth floor entrance led me to Javier, the manager, who could not have been more welcoming and helpful. Rooms are well-outfitted and comfortable, either facing a quiet courtyard or a vibrant, well-lit urban plaza. For my next visit to Madrid, I will return to the Acapulco without hesitation, because it feels like my home away from home!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Bilal Qureshi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW4u2eKpnN1MySM5pqCuBemRQVWpN8kLSzgN5U5klbrkHgqRKkq=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I came across recommendations for this hotel due to best location chose it.

Our experience was fantastic. We toured everything by foot, no hassle with taxis or public transport. Most of the well-known historic places and top museums are within walking distance - wonderful!

Moreover, there are great options to eat, shop etc nearby. This small family-run hotel is perfectly located for tourists in Madrid.

Front desk is available 24/7 and very helpful with typical tourist questions.

Complimentary coffee, collection of teas is available; also a fridge in the room and a common microwave to warm up stuff. Additional drinks or small snacks like chocolates can be purchased anytime.

Highly recommended for its location, nice-helpful staff, clean and functional room - great value for money!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"mollytrip123","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QftlnsLB8VReEIa8q3x-FUIWL9jAVnw9OiPzFml0xLx7k1DporKgZdMXGALflbNzntF3w=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Fabulous small hotel. Room was exceptional and staff were very helpful. Quiet room, balcony, great facilities, great beds. Very clean and well maintained, highly recommended. No stairs, lift access. Also good value for money, superb location. Close to Gran Via metro and Plaza Major just a short walk. Safe area/security building with 24/7 front desk","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: LATROUPE Prado Hostel Madrid
address: Paseo de las Delicias, 1, Arganzuela, 28045 Madrid
descriptions: Informal dorms & private rooms, some with balconies, in a casual hostel with a restaurant & a bar.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Kristine Sweigart","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJPMb8htMWyn5DAMQIxAfTg86Ods4ss-n7L3mM5q2AuTQkEK9PL=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We stayed here in a private room for two nights after arriving in Madrid because we wanted to have easy access to the train station which was pretty much across the street. The room had an great skylight that you can open with a view of the agriculture ministry which was lit up at night. The room was clean and was really nicely decorated. The bed was comfortable but the shower let water go everywhere. Breakfast was OK but not worth the $10. We paid for it because we wanted the convenience of not trying to find food before the early morning trains since most restaurants are closed that early. Hindsight being 20/20, we could have grabbed something at the train station. The room itself was slightly expensive but worth it for the location.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rachel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLPbf8J22_QFUlyPTg4VjAm94FX3SJ5zCJRw29afkbke_mOTg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I stayed in this hostel for one night and had a fantastic experience! Right from check in, the staff were so friendly and helpful. Though they knew I spoke English, they asked/offered to speak in Spanish to help me practice if I wanted, which I did! I arrived early and was able to use the storage lockers. I stayed in a 4 bed all female dorm. The room was clean, spacious and had large locker drawers for each person. I participated in the Saturday night pub crawl - so much fun and met awesome people!!! The location is great, not right in the center so its quieter, but still close enough to walk everywhere and super close to the Atocha station. Breakfast was amazing. I will definitely stay here again! Book in advance for best value.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Audun Ingebrigtsen","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWC1oaSkWAd76RCMRU_1EhwnvLRHcbXjwE8xpkbO2ipP2puu0Yd=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Very close to the Atocha train station if you need a night while traveling through Madrid.

The showers are nice and it’s a modern and good looking rooms. If you got a very big suitcase it won’t fit the personal locker. Some rooms it’s not a problem with space in the room for it though. Like the 7-bedroom dorm I was in last time. The 8-bedroom dorm it worked but was not optimal.

There should have been a sink in the room where the toilet is too. And it would also be good if the part of the room with showes and toilet had a door or something between so you easier could shower, brush your teeth etc. and disturbed the others less.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"ZAC creatives","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW8aoCPGripmLYalrLTai7_YGx1Dp_x88YphiieJ3PbEv8Na_fb=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I being only one night. Definitely a place for young people.
the reception was really nice. they offer you even a phone number to call if you need something or have a doubt.
breakfast was OK.
if I have to point something negative is that I would like a kettle in the private room for make a tea at least. and the heater was locked and was impossible for me setting it a bit more up. in general lines I recommend it.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"mjrc2025","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QftlnsLB8VReEIa8q3x-FUIWL9jAVnw9OiPzFml0xLx7k1DporKgZdMXGALflbNzntF3w=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Check in was good|Location good|Atmosphere is good|Room was small for family of four. They pick personal fights with people. They canceled my reservation at the last minute, without any reason, when I arrived at Madrid airport, and offered me separate accommodation without refunding my prepayment. If I was able to stay here for four days, why didn't they refund my prepayment? The holiday I've been wanting for a long time started thanks to this hostel. If you pay regularly, you won't have any problems. Very few of the staff are polite. They are very rude and don't give any guidance. This hostel is a money-strapping, terrible place. It seems corporate!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Ally","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLAk-MBETdPOt_U8Do1Lm5EuEaDgPfiS2-Ff_x888Rw2NQ_EQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"My stay at Latroupe Hostel Madrid was fine overall. The location is convenient and the hostel has a modern look, mixed dorm bedroom had a bit of a weird smell to it though. The pub crawl was enjoyable, good value for the money, and a good opportunity to meet other travellers from other hostels.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Josie Middleton","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK0GDlwXlGL7OiVJME2gk7Hw4UdPKsZMHJ1scCKfC9v1j1Vxw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Very disappointed in this hostel, specifically the restaurant. We decided to come here for NYE as a group and the reception was great. They greeted us with kindness and explained all the rules and gave us suggestions for the surrounding area.

The restaurant POLAR OPPOSITE. They looked annoyed to see us during happy hour as if we did not spend a decent amount of money there. We went on NYE, and they told us that they were not doing the happy hour that was advertised and we were told at check in (see picture below). 3 waiters told us different things, complete mess. We tried to order a jug of sangria as we were a party of 10 and we were told that we were confusing places and that they don’t do that when we just had the drink THE DAY PRIOR in the bar. Finally we decided to leave and the lady looked at us and told us “next time you could be more nice”. As someone who has worked 8+ years in the food and service industry, they should be embarrassed by this service. Never would I ever treat paying customers who bring revenue to a business like this. For this reason also I would never recommend this place to anyone.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD8Pwok36KJgSjNdV6n686t&fid=0x0:0x260dc1d0a75bde19"],"When":"2025-12-31"}]

We asked for a simple email with directions on how to get there via the subway system (which is very direct) . They sent an email with “see attachment “ but there was no attachment! We arrived at 12:15 Am and missed the office check in even after buzzing and buzzing what we thought was the intercom. The hotel is poorly identified and once someone let us in, there were no clear instructions as to where the office was. None of the other guests knew the telephone number of whom to phone. At 1:00 am we walked a few blocks around the corner and got a different hotel room which was far nicer. title: Hostal Santa Barbara
address: Plaza de Sta. Bárbara, 4, Centro, 28004 Madrid
descriptions: Simply furnished rooms in a straightforward hotel featuring complimentary Wi-Fi.

title: FAR HOME ATOCHA
address: C. de Atocha, 45, Centro, 28012 Madrid
descriptions: Trendy property offering basic dorms & private rooms, plus a common kitchen & a terrace.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"simon gurney","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUH0S2otUD8kg4NolSPl6fSNVbzeSYmTprxpssq2PFdXXC3PjQ1=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Outstanding location, perfectly located in the heart of Madrid, a short walk to everything of interest. Nice setup, the common area and the kitchen are spacious and comfortable, very convenient and there's plenty of big supermarkets near for those preferring to eat at the hostel. Also a great Churro place just at the end of the road.
Nice outside patio area next to the common area. Overall everything is clean and well maintained. Lots of bathrooms and the showers were great.
Decent room, basic but clean and functional.
Hard to find better value accommodation in such a great location without everything a budget traveller needs for a comfortable stay.
While we were staying they were doing a free coffee and churros morning, free guided city walk and movie night, so definitely making the effort to provide a good hostel experience.
The elevator music throughout the hostel didn't do much for me, I prefer the silence. The piano in the common area is quite annoying when someone decides to start playing badly at 10 pm.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Helen S","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLVlok1SkEC8sQw21E9V9B40QofWkny5GTpdI8j7gilz7bNwTj3=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This was a great first hostel experience for me! title: Hostal Nersan
address: C. de la Flor Baja, 5, Centro, 28013 Madrid
descriptions: Streamlined rooms with a funky vibe in an unpretentious budget hotel offering complimentary Wi-Fi.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Esteve Soria","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVQTjd85HgO_0Zf7smgt1EZwNHdOJ6V0fGeWf4trK9l0L_dYaVxqA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"UPDATE: 3 months later they have not refunded the money yet.

I booked for two nights and when I got there the room (nº 1) had black mold on the bathroom ceiling and water in the floor.
I called the contact phone and I was informed that they knew about the problem but there was no other room available. They offered me a change to another room for the day after but I should stay in that room for the day. That was not acceptable as that room should have not  been offered at all in the state it was.

I contacted the booking platform and they offered an alternative so we went somewhere else","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDtwMPV9gE&fid=0x0:0x27f0438aab25dfc1"],"When":"2024-2-1"},{"Name":"travellerine","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QeqQESQs2CRf2VRyln1Raq0k2FSdB19mGKme5Xh6nHXcs2V7pTrOayJAdxC-qWdu9VyTA=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"We booked this hostel with no high expectations, knowing what kind of horrific hostel accomodation central Madrid can offer. This was a pleasant surprise! We arrived at midnight and had no problem getting in, there is always someone there to answer the door and the phone. We were 3 girls and got a room with 3 separate beds at the end of the hallway, incl. night tables, cupboard for clothes and a mirror, also a ceiling fan which was a huge relief during hot summer nights. The window opened to the side street, which was a bit noisy (being right in the very HEART of the city) but we all had earplugs, so sleeping was not a problem. There were 2 toilets and 2 showers on the hallway, toilets always had paper and were cleaned every day. Clean towels were given for no extra charge and were changed upon request. We stayed for a long weekend (3nights) and paid about 14euros each/night, peanuts! The hostel is located right next to Gran Via, 2 min from metro stop Plaza de España, about 5-10min walk from SOL. For this price we honestly could not have asked for more. It was easy to drop off shopping bags and go right back out, without waisting any time. There is a great Grill restaurant right aroung the corner where you get a whole load of meat on "parilla" cold wine and nice salads for about 20€ per person, loved it! Would def stay at this hostel again as there was really nothing to complain about! And most importantly - NO CUCARACHAS seen during our stay, very unusual! :)))","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"littlemissfez1983","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0Qc1kNGO8LfCIUkNiXrsfeYG3onj8HWsGiBwC71th43hKBnE5GlvQyCYmZ1-b-eE7ReWRw=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Admittedly it is not fancy but I found it to be clean, cool and completely adequate for our needs. We had a double room with a private bathroom which was directly opposite the reception so I was worried it might be noisy but we didn't hear anybody else while in the room. The location is great being very close to Gran Via and Plaza de Espana and the staff were all very nice. The only issue we had was the door to the bathroom scraped across the floor and made an unpleasant noise but we once we worked out that lifting the door a little avoided this it was all good.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"TravellerDreamerMore","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QeCck1CfANpZm9bBk3PHtP97Kh9k25HZfvq9yAWlN3BaIyjSHUPEfWkhAJmuYiJsH-_6g=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"The room could be fine but was dirty and full of hair everywhere and on the floor in every corner. |The shower was dark inside and the toilette product were very poor. |My tarif included a welcome drink but I forgot it and they didn't give me anything.|When I reported it at the check out the lady said they she was not there and just took note.|The rooms are on a floor of a private building which as no lights and in the evening is not nice to enter because it is open to everyone (No key, No code).||Avoid it!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"imogenc421","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QfndCAq-uxCGOZMI7LGh3BMpWGEbPhcwmoTu71Rum6FWmIVCdty-rocNNYz1q8SL9qX-Q=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Stayed this weekend and this place must have been totally redone since the previous reviews. We stayed in a twin room, but had a peek into other rooms as they weren't all occupied and they are all modern and nicely decorated and each has their own bathroom. It is located on the fourth floor of an apartment building on a quiet side street between two Japanese restaurants, you just buzz up to the hotel when you arrive. The owner speaks no English but was extremely patient with our terrible Spanish and always very friendly. You are given keys and can come and go as you please. There is free wifi which came in handy. The only downsides were the following:||- The check out time is 12 and check in was from 12.30. Unsurprisingly they were still cleaning the room when we arrived promptly at 12.30, but they let us freshen up and when we came back from lunch at 2 it was all ready for us||- There was only 1 plug socket in the room which was not by the mirror, but there were 2 in the bathroom which we also used||- There was a rail to hang some clothes on but no drawers or wardrobe||||Overall the price was brilliant for such a lovely, comfortable, centrally located room and I would definitely stay here again and recommend it to friends.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"lolam459","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0Qc1kNGO8LfCIUkNiXrsfeYG3onj8HWsGiBwC71th43hKBnE5GlvQyCYmZ1-b-eE7ReWRw=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Quiet place and not expensive at all. I booked through Hostelworld.com, but there was some initial confusion because the hostel deducted money from me and then asked me to pay again upon arrival. Later, Hostelworld refunded the deducted amount. So just be aware: if you book through Hostelworld, you may be charged once initially and then asked to pay again at the hostel before receiving the refund.
Aside from that, everything else was great. Booking directly through the hostel’s website seems much easier and smoother. The staff were generally very cooperative and helpful. The morning breakfast was amazing, the view was beautiful, and the location is quite nice.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tanmoy Ahmed Chowdhury","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUcZJENbmETpc99qYchExZQL5zO3uomW_7tRWIvnIMgOPpll8U=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I had an absolutely wonderful stay at this hostel and honestly couldn’t have asked for a better experience. I stayed in the dormitory, and the bed was incredibly comfortable—one of the best I’ve slept on while traveling. It made a huge difference after long, tiring days.
The facilities were outstanding: clean, well-organized, and thoughtfully designed to make guests feel relaxed and at home. Everything worked smoothly, and the atmosphere was welcoming and positive.

A very special mention goes to the receptionist, Lili. She is truly amazing. Whenever I needed anything—big or small—she was always there to help with a smile and genuine care. Her kindness and professionalism made my stay even more memorable and stress-free.
This hostel doesn’t just offer a place to sleep; it offers comfort, support, and a feeling of belonging.

I would happily stay here again and highly recommend it to anyone looking for a great hostel experience.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Gloria Notaro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLDqh1gDds_mc46tnfaKqfaWFE9ChjhxUJrh-dreqZIvl1hUA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A very nice hostel — small, cozy, and welcoming. The beds are comfortable and all areas are kept perfectly clean. The location is excellent, just next to a major metro and train station, which makes getting around very easy. The staff is also super friendly and helpful.

The 8-bed dormitory is a bit small, but you still get plenty of privacy thanks to the curtains and the bed-cube design. Overall, a great stay. Highly recommended!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Abigail","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXcagJbpYzSiai4uRwSL3la62SeSaeUm9Nj7Y3zqaH_KHyJVqIHVw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This hostel is run by AI there are no humans in this hostel. You enter by putting your sensitive information in a kiosk outside of the hostel, so make sure you have data/wifi. I did this and arrived after checkout time (4 pm), yet the system would not give me my key card, saying that “the bed wasn’t ready”. So I clicked the help button, which connects to a call center in a far away foreign country.  The first time, a woman picked up and said she’s “helping other customers” and she hung up on me, it took 20 more minutes for anyone to answer. A man picked up the call and when I said hello he said “ you better be nice or else I’m not going to help you” I was too stunned to speak, it was pouring rain and all I said was hola. Eventually he realized the mistake and fixed it, I was able to check in. Well it got worse. When you enter the building you have to go down stairs, and there is no elevator, so be prepared if you have heavy bags. I was fortunate to get put in a female dorm, however when I opened the door there was a broken full glass mirror all over the floor. Glass shards were everywhere. I immediately used WhatsApp to tell the ai chat bot, who told me it will transfer it to a human. One hour later, they told me they will not fix anything as “it’s not their fault” . I begged them saying there is glass in the bathroom, shower, everywhere, that this cannot be safe. I asked for compensation and they refused. Well after begging and pleading they sent a human to clean up the glass.

Outside of this tumultuous entry, it seems they have humans clean the rooms every few hours. It is clean, and whatever material they use for the floors dries water quite quickly so the showers and bathroom was fine. They came in about 4 times a day to do random things like change key cards and clean the common bathroom. Because my dorm only had 2-3 people at a time, it was manageable but I would not stay here if it was fully booked, because the walkway is quite narrow and there are no windows if it gets too hot. They do give a free towel (most hostels dont so this was a nice touch), and there is no need to bring a padlock, because your keycard will do it for you.There is no luggage storage. With all this considered, I want you to take a good look at yourself and say is this something I want to do. This is not a social hostel, it’s honestly more of a capsule hotel. I think they should market it like that, and make it more apparent when booking it is run by AI.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Daniela W. Pelton","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVzIv0w90n6f0lR-jeOkKts_Z5wmOnn7h5XKAmO6b_4Jk8Y1tqM-Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I don't understand why this place has high reviews. I've stayed in MANY hostels in many different countries over the last 15 years. But never did I cut my stay short and just forgo the money I paid. But that is how poorly I slept my first night in this hostel. What I don't understand is how you can stuff 8 people into a tiny room with no open window and absolutely no air conditioning at night. I felt like I was suffocating in a coffin. Mind you, this is mid October when there was lovely fresh air at night, but there was no way to open a window because all the rooms are underground. So even though it was cool and crisp outside, it was absolutely BOILING inside the room the entire night. I maybe slept 2 out of the 8 hours I attempted. By early morning, I knew I had to go. I asked if they'd refund me for the 2nd night I paid for, but they refused. I highly DO NOT RECOMMEND unless you enjoy sleeping in sauna-like temperatures. The only pro is I liked the central location, and the Peruvian girl who greeted me at the door had a lovely personality.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Valentina Russano","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL0zJqpvNRAkqnmc9cfZGJjCHaRuCu6o_zZeei85QS4VsunLw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"horrible experience, i got there after midnight because my flight got in late and you have to self check in (no reception) went into my room and there was someone sleeping on my bed, complained at reception the next morning and they didn’t even care. weird smells, beds not private enough, extremely small rooms.
don’t stay here, save your money","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Maria joão Mascarenhas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUjHjs-Fvx02Iq_I6moPkS9ZrOO-Sb-fSry1I6mjoOXlYcd_Ycj9g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"This hostel is ok if you don’t plan on spending more than 2 nights in there. Even though the location is good and the beds are fairly comfortable, it is impossible to shower decently. The shower simply has no pressure, I could barely rinse the shower gel, let alone wash my hair. On my opinion the quality does not justify the price.","Images":null,"When":""}]

Take the slide down!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDTXKFaDAMvt_TxkkmUMJ5T&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-12-29"},{"Name":"Ana Reyes","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWMwtoVpk1i3x_wiRJTSSM-Q4T6tR8QfucPFB3sfTx613SCfoKg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"1/5 stars*

Visited The Sweet Space museum in Madrid with my family, and unfortunately, it was a disappointing experience. We were expecting a fun and engaging time, but what we got was quite the opposite.

Firstly, the duration of the experience was misleading. They claimed it would be around 40-50 minutes long, but in reality, we were done in less than 15 minutes. It felt like a rushed and overpriced experience.

The attractions themselves were also quite boring. My kids and I didn't find them engaging or entertaining, and it seemed like they were just going through the motions.

The only highlights of our visit were the toboggan and the pool full of plastic balls. My kids seemed to enjoy those parts, but it wasn't enough to make up for the rest of the experience. My kids started crying ad they were looking for more things to do but by that time we were already by the exit.

Overall, I wouldn't recommend The Sweet Space museum to others. It felt like a scam, and the lack of transparency about the experience duration was frustrating. Maybe they can work on improving their attractions and being more honest about what visitors can expect.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCYATxwx6h_tORsyiHHhdMY&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-8-17"},{"Name":"Deborah Laks","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI3grBV_SHuXFTJao3Rutge7Qtph1-vhD5tPBYjbJL4SV7esFjt=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A modern museum with a childhood twist. A nice place to enjoy with your kids.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAPsDf66iV5GVDHHBkIymuV&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-10-28"},{"Name":"Maria Manduca","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLp8c_vw_Rx8IXWepmHa2TGsRjR-x5UtvczrqYHPDzQrUiybA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We spent a different day. [{"Name":"Can","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVNslvhVRdHS5a2DPOldz7fU8nbA3R7JngSGWMd6SbjHvobHpr8=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is the most amazing museum I've ever seen. Everyone working there is very polite. The museum is incredibly detailed and has particularly fantastic fossils. The price is very, very reasonable for a place like this. Definitely worth a visit.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDMaaPLmAy-5IIsZKz9fBvM&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2026-2-11"},{"Name":"Syed Jilani","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXmWDI7kflGQbOE_B-gKNBzjNx8IJRO-qeeYZEawZymUa7PPmIV=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Visiting Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales feels less like walking into a museum and more like stepping into a carefully curated adventure through time. From the very first hall, you’re surrounded by giants of the natural world — towering dinosaur skeletons, massive whale bones suspended overhead, and displays that instantly spark a childlike sense of wonder, no matter your age.

What makes this museum special is how it balances scale with storytelling. The fossil galleries don’t just show ancient creatures; they invite you to imagine their lives, their habitats, and the dramatic changes that shaped our planet. One moment you’re staring up at a prehistoric predator, and the next you’re face-to-face with meticulously preserved mammals, birds, and insects that highlight the incredible diversity of life on Earth.

The evolution and biodiversity sections are especially engaging. They gently guide you through complex scientific ideas without ever feeling heavy or overwhelming. Interactive elements and well-written explanations keep things lively, making it easy to lose track of time as you move from ecosystems to human evolution. There’s a quiet thrill in realizing how deeply connected everything is — from microscopic organisms to the largest animals that ever lived.

The building itself adds to the experience. Grand yet welcoming, it has that classic museum charm where history lingers in the air, but the exhibits feel fresh and thoughtfully updated. Families, solo travelers, and curious minds all seem equally at home here.

By the time you leave, you don’t just feel informed — you feel inspired. The Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales is an adventurous journey through nature’s past and present, one that reminds you how astonishing, fragile, and endlessly fascinating our world truly is.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Moses Jhon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVTMzTmKx317bL25VfaSo0yD1VlLLd7gxxf7uMu8Tzd1BCEUzU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"s there a better way to spark your curiosity than stepping into a world where dinosaurs roam and ancient ecosystems come alive? MNCN in Madrid is a treasure trove of natural wonders that had me hooked from the first fossil. The interactive exhibits, especially the human evolution display, make you feel like a scientist on a discovery mission. Pro tip: grab tickets online for €7 (free for kids under 4 or seniors over 65) to skip the queues, and start on the left side of the building for the best flow. Oh, and the African elephant in the lobby? It’s been standing tall since 1929, a silent guardian of the museum’s epic story. Bring comfy shoes and a water bottle—you’ll want to linger for hours","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCsBanB9aY1UYEiIrOQx7g_&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2025-7-15"},{"Name":"Summer A.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWkStxgtyPwdVIJ1k6zDUe9ZikrcD65XzzmzgOE4A98xhEtpMT3xw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently visited the Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales in Madrid and was genuinely impressed. It’s the kind of place that strikes the right balance — fun and engaging for kids, but just as interesting for adults. There’s also a cafe just nearby to relax afterwards. Everything in the museum is written and explained both in Spanish and English so it’s very tourist-friendly.

One of the most striking things is getting to see life-size replicas of animals, from elephants to whales to dinosaurs. You can stare a tiger or a gorilla straight in the face — something you wouldn’t be able to do in the wild without putting yourself in serious danger. It really gives you a sense of their scale and presence.

There was a great exhibit on the history of dinosaurs. And one of my favorite parts was a photo exhibition showing animals in funny, human-like poses — funny and cleverly done. The exhibit explained how seeing animals this way can help people empathize with them, which in turn can raise awareness and motivate action to protect their habitats. Thoughtful, while also pretty entertaining.

Another interesting part of the museum is an exhibit dedicated to Santiago Ramón y Cajal, the Spanish neuroscientist who won a Nobel Prize for his work on the structure of the nervous system. The way his research is displayed feels almost like peeking into the early days of modern science — delicate drawings, old instruments, and lots of context. You can also see some of his photography - an interest he had on the side.

There’s also a beautiful old room that used to be part of a private natural history collection from a former mayor. It still has that “cabinet of curiosities” vibe — dark wood, antique display cases, and a mix of fossils, minerals, and preserved animals. It’s like stepping back in time, and it adds a really charming contrast to the more modern parts of the museum.

All in all, it’s a really worthwhile visit — part science, part history, and part wonder. Whether you’re with kids or just feeling curious yourself, there’s a lot to explore and enjoy.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB8fMKsFQsgujaIWQq8mUUR&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2025-7-16"},{"Name":"Michael Currin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWP39nsOd4LZSJSXbjuEyg2bRgo__eldMdZfh1xXPuWucYXmsTVVg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The fossil collection of dinosaurs and related animals was impressive.
Also enjoyed the section on dinosaurs to birds with the taxidermied birds.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBbDXTdlaDJF0yAUtAS2Se0&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2026-1-6"},{"Name":"Anthony Kraag","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIBVOrMEqTU4Ng7DSUYYRPbIEnGvv6fNYdSjvT3okp4F-Wuww=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales offers an impressive collection of stuffed animals, including an elephant, lion, and tiger, all beautifully preserved and displayed. The dinosaur fossils were another highlight—fascinating to see up close! Furthermore, there was a stunning photo exhibition showcasing beautiful images of animals, captured with incredible detail (by Tim Flach)
The exhibits are well-organized and educational, making it a great place for both kids and adults. The museum has a classic, old-school charm but still feels engaging. Plus, it's very affordable!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMDAotX3zAE&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2025-2-7"},{"Name":"SM Othman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWITlDmHJnZaPhLNddQ8ra0b3AZAvQP2-PpjTBAW4RsjS-SoHxa=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently visited the Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales in Madrid, and it was an incredible experience! The museum offers a fascinating journey through the natural world, with a wide range of exhibits covering everything from paleontology and geology to biodiversity and ecosystems.

The dinosaur skeletons and fossils were particularly impressive, captivating visitors of all ages. The displays were well-curated, informative, and engaging, making it easy to learn while enjoying the visuals. The museum is also very family-friendly, with interactive sections that are perfect for kids.

The building itself is beautiful, and the staff were welcoming and helpful throughout my visit. Whether you’re a science enthusiast or just looking for an educational and entertaining activity in Madrid, this museum is a must-visit. I highly recommend taking your time to explore all the exhibits—there’s so much to see and learn!

I’ll definitely be coming back next time I’m in Madrid!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDv8J68ygE&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2024-12-17"},{"Name":"Carlos Melgar Castillo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVK4eXDd_2Rd_9e64PgSMjfdygc-r_b0wbPtdjNNs3kcDK-fuCxFg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Entry fees: (August 2024)

- Free entry on Sundays from 17:00 -20:00 hours.
- Normal entry 7 euros.

Spectacular museum!!

Great for people who love science and biology and people who can appreciate biodiversity and care about endangered species.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICr48qxKw&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2024-7-10"}]

user_reviews: [{"Name":"G Mac","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfFY2KM7hfNlooonhvV8_u_Q6GiEzn9xdqejVwqehpMdOIKlCRug=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"An absolutely stunning museum explaining the history of Madrid. It's a short tour and the host is wonderful!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCoFqxGxLjfoKDn60NDsRyv&fid=0x0:0xbfa93202fc6d3d55"],"When":"2026-3-9"},{"Name":"Leslie Sepulveda","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJQtR5gLIfHvnuvtMZCnzpou_RZjVN1ArRA_AVttnZtAjdv1w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rossana Rioperez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKHpteVLxVl7EhTRq6OqI1EdiHGsCRR2PdBv-4zbcSSRr603A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Una visita súper interesante a este nuevo museo en Madrid. Ideal para pasar con niños un rato didáctico.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Manuel Gutierrez Hernandez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXBhNgNShCgkXUUDaMsWqDfvucTT-e5pjyDFfDEBzL13Aw_47pu=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Muy interesante, el staff muy amable, pequeño pero merece la pena","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhATsOKMdqiOvYznF-9XpxuY&fid=0x0:0xbfa93202fc6d3d55"],"When":"2026-3-7"},{"Name":"Lucía Fernández","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWo-7rJUvFax_U_UfEomhs-QAw3KDdofD-gwXZ6CPawE0ORnTuGVw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El museo de Leonardo Da Vinci está muy bien, sobre todo cuando te lo explican. Las dos personas que nos hicieron la visita lo hicieron genial y así se disfruta mucho más.

Hay maquetas de máquinas e inventos en sus cuadernos de madera hechas a partir de los dibujos de Leonardo y también reproducciones de la Mona Lisa y La Última Cena.

Echo de menos que haya más pantallas o elementos interactivos para entender mejor cómo funcionan las máquinas. Aun así, la visita merece la pena.

Por otro lado pensábamos con tanto vinilo que estaban en obras, porque parecía que estaba cerrado y al abrir la puerta no vimos la taquilla.
Hay descuentos para niños y mayores.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC6JgZvhlyijm5ZQpihWU4U&fid=0x0:0xbfa93202fc6d3d55"],"When":"2026-3-6"}]

user_reviews: [{"Name":"Lorenzo Berardinelli","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLQYZfs7aGmPhWV6GArevvBQrV3lL4dmCAQU_1872nRZD-NgA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I visited the Museum of Illusions in Madrid with my girlfriend, and it was a fun experience overall. However, I think it’s more enjoyable if you go in a group of more than two people, since many of the photo spots are designed for pairs or groups. Inside, there aren’t that many interactive activities, but the main issue is definitely the number of children running around — though of course, you can’t really ask them not to come! Other than that, we had a good time. I’d recommend going with friends or people you know you can have fun with anywhere.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDmml8-lSdI7MJDcz6dCc1F\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-10-27"},{"Name":"Darren Beck","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLRfTQ5lzSivZGEcb4EWCur2uNoffJUe10rBNwzOdNobY-mUg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A little small but worth an hour or so if you want a break from the heat. Note that it definitely skews more toward a younger crowd, and there were a lot of families with little kids there. As a group of two adults though, it was still a fun experience. I’m sure the kids had a great time as well. Staff were great also. Give it a try if you need a break from the sightseeing and just want some fun relaxation and air conditioning!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCDZBKUvmAhb_LOAn8QK7LQ\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-13"},{"Name":"rated by maryam • food critic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV2rFU889_uyIosz_7bpffSFpmpz0-gKcaVKqZvHb7h1qD_qhXV=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Absolutely entertaining from start to finish! The Museum of Illusions in Madrid kept us all fully engaged — we could’ve easily spent two hours exploring. The installations are clever, interactive, and truly high quality. My kids had a blast, and honestly, so did I. Every room is picture perfect, and the staff made sure the flow was easy and fun. Whether you’re going with kids or just want to play with perspective and be wowed, this spot delivers.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCvIn2cbOwD1xlT2Bju8D5l\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-7-1"},{"Name":"Alex S","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWuqMxZHWW4Q3oWg1MozJ5EDSP48AY-vf9ImsmDzcUsLKEKjbGa=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wow! What a great museum! If you want to find where the magic is at in Madrid, look no further! Offering many illusions for you to try out and capture, this museum is incredibly fun for everyone of all ages except people who otherwise have vertigo, as some of the illusions you will have to walk through may not be good for people with vertigo.\nIf you are wanting a fun activity to do with your kids, it’s a great place! Depending on your pace, you could be at this museum for a while. My favourite illusion was the vortex, as it is a really cool experience and it really makes you feel like you are spinning in an actual vortex. If that’s your thing, it is the last illusion downstairs at the very end of the room. This museum is also not far from the train line, so you can travel here easily.\nIn sum, if you’re wanting something to do while you’re in Madrid, come here!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAiCzHJZBmXN2Y3VDQxPYol\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-7-7"},{"Name":"Michael Stockdale","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIIrWLzicQpgC3hAFUv5MqBQNE3eX1_sKxt7Pa3MR8iF60jyQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"It was okay but l was hoping to see more than l did for the entry price.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCoNl4gi2TxojBRMWk7xy-N\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-12-15"},{"Name":"Rebecca Sirois","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUVZzU3H_Gmj---yW_cc8WSqFd5G5oCZ4-hT-20m9bPEiVF3i3R=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Such a fun experience with the kids. Definitely buy tickets in advance, they don't let you in early so show up right on time. Staff were helpful for picture taking otherwise a fun place to wander through at your leisure.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCS7-Y2SbFgXGvCL3e_1CBg\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-29"},{"Name":"Joel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWl5821tyzJrwoJ9WtZ4ckHFtm77vetb8svyUaCTvs_yjbDKrzH=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We had such a great time at the Museum of Illusions in Madrid! I went with my two kids and they absolutely loved it — from the mind-bending exhibits to all the fun photo ops, there was never a dull moment. A big shoutout to Roberto, who went above and beyond. He was incredibly kind and patient, explaining how the illusions worked in a way my kids could understand, and even helped us take some amazing photos. He showed us the best angles and made sure we got the most out of each exhibit. Definitely recommend for a fun, family-friendly outing. So much fun and lots of laughs!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA41UAbda0q4GHtxLgczpgq\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-29"},{"Name":"David Sargent","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXNd7Vx1pxVZ2hd91K_Xk_NBWo4ZUDgz8hEItPBONm_A7r1mRme=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I love finding unusual museums, and I read about this one on WikiTravel when I had some spare time on a recwnt business trip. This museum was an absolute delight! My only regret was that I was alone, as a lot of the displays are interactive and the couples and groups were having so much fun watching and photographing each other and srading places and such that I decided that someday I need to go back there with at least one other person who hasn't seen it before. Fingers crossed for another trip to Madrid one of these days!!","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"G Mac","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfFY2KM7hfNlooonhvV8_u_Q6GiEzn9xdqejVwqehpMdOIKlCRug=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a fantastic building with some wonderful artworks. The ground floor has some student works many of true quality and talent. You descend one floor to see an exhibition of graphic arts from the early 1900s on. You descend to the main exhibition space which has a moving collection of work depiction the trauma of immigration and the victims of this dangerous journey via sea.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAKVHdLqOJeu4cq3_Ew79dC&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2025-9-24"},{"Name":"S Bristol","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI0zVxZqdB273yxQV1VinjzNJsA0sZ28LlFSrl4rEY9BlkzSg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"An excellent museum in a lively neighborhood with many independent cafés, bars and restaurants. A history of the early Spanish illustrated magazine  ' White and Black ', showing many covers and early cartoons in chronological order from 1901. Varied artistic illustrations showcasing the ' modern life', politics etc. Other exhibitions on more floors. We visited towards the end of the day,  and could have easily spent 2 hours there, plus visiting the cafe. Recommended!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDzmOedbw&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2024-6-7"},{"Name":"Marc G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK2hJP8h7COFWvZMrwQPcpLDair4JLlEimwAnddDETKQNGIvV5s=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Loved the Museum and the (free!) guided tour. Wonderful to go from 1890ish all the way to 2000 in history through caricature, illustration and art… don’t miss out on visiting this place! I can confirm that very young & upwards will enjoy this colourful and fun exhibition. Most pieces are driven by action & personality of the subjects, or by the relatable situation of the historic moment they were created in.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDTkKuJOQ&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2024-5-21"},{"Name":"Will Finley","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVE9RXbySJxSR7Ize1IBsmiQkteh52ZtMoGsP7LqIwsVeMif9Er=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Not so much a museum as it is a book store. Which by itself is amazing. It is the largest selection of design books I've seen in one place. Very interesting stuff from all over the world with beautiful design. The building is also fantastic. The metal panel facade is striking and the underlit floor modules are really cool looking at night. They gate off the courtyard when it gets dark though. The museum is not exceptionally inspiring, but you should still go up to the second level to walk through the connector.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDE_-TNHg&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2017-6-14"},{"Name":"Elisabetta Borzini","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWW9OaJFHYeOlmWeyc9v25DLabeFhCxN7jRlbMj8RkcjNuLAtMf=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"So, the museum is actually closed but the exhibition on the ground floor is open (and free).
It’s an exhibition about transgender people, trans art and trans artists. I didn’t expect it and I’m so grateful we stumbled open it because it was so heartwarming and marvellous. Thank you for this space!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICFjay90wE&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2023-10-30"},{"Name":"Christian Jimenez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKJschGurXRAeIT0xo2IXQDdEoBMGg9nvZnG8j56pfgoYf7jg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Free Museum featuring Illustration work as well as some digital work. user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Dong Cai","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUVDzBsXATZ4chn3Go4FUKsm03lk7jcwsyJ89uTw1eNKA2ebF_c=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"The experience in itself is good.  However, given the small size of the venue and things to do, definitely not worth the 68€ for a family of 5.  Everything could be done and seen in literally 15-20 mins.  Definitely felt like a tourist trap for this venue.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Stefan Nilles","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVcSebPbMGAP-j9hMDMpSkJM2OSZiScSw7Jd2G1MSWm5hmuLuIdjw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Do not visit this place unless your are a Spanish nationalist family with very young children!

The place is catering exclusively to children to teach them about the embroidery on the torrreros' uniform, the tradition of spanish tiles as an art from and other mundane details of Spanish culture that are presented as something Spanish kids should be proud of.

The glacially paced narrative of the projected information is in Spanish (for 3 year olds) and you can read the English translation projected to the wall.

Total waste of money and time.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBxSdMF_EItVZ0NhATVpwun&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-11-18"},{"Name":"Chris Tomkins","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUMdh9K_6GffzOUidLsxnoeKD-7jw1e7YUvNARYkIb04iAytgI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Really good set up. Very engaging immersive experience for the artists. The bit at the end was just novel fun.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCHot09waWMZrBDGpS-w-Z3&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-7-28"},{"Name":"Jonas Montvidas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXykj1DBG41wM8onrJXAQ3usyMsllep8989BjgjWIqriCxo8UQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"It is a hoax. You pay 18 euros to get into two rooms - one bigger and one smaller. The same story about The Last Supper by Davinci goes on a loop in the bigger room, and there are two/three activities for the children in the smaller room. Two short hallways with lights are the main attraction of this place. But even these hallways are just a cheap copy of other museums worldwide that offer better experiences. Not worth 18 euros","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCoqoaZkAE&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-4-16"},{"Name":"Shalom N","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXjmGFJHDuC19RcGGg0lG7mHAePo2CjbG7fd4Pk9sPEsG8DxAUj=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"It was cute for the 10 minutes it lasted for, you can tell that this place was made for influencers to come and take a quick video so others will feel the need to come.

It was one main exhibition room where they just projected some stuff on the wall, then another room where you get to view terrible VR and do some coloring, i got pretty pictures i guess but would i recommend? No because its not giving you value for your money

It is giving very much tourist trap because you leave feeling unsatisfied because it can literally take you 5 minutes to go through it all.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCQ58b0UQ&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-3-7"},{"Name":"Jose Maria Tijerina","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUN2vlCdnJoVVzCMvczORJZNy6fp097DcqnE5mvO5JL7UyEJ4mK=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The immersive experience is very interesting. When I visited, the experience was about the work of Klimt and Van Gogh. I went on a Monday so it was not full. The extra activities besides the immersive experience are more for kids than adults. There are a  virtual painting wall, last for 3 minutes, drawings for kids and virtual reality headsets that were not working when i attended. The narration on the immersive experience is ominous Spanish language, so if you cannot follow it then you would not get  part of the experience.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCQHraYDrmqxt2VPIpl0y2E&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-7-13"},{"Name":"Ariannaly Pea Martínez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUHlslcBXwCCEYwj-22n12iqaNzJJSfd6xSJOjFHvdkhRKV2mc=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"It’s a pretty nice and neat experience. Visuals and music were the best of it, although the interactive room was a bit mid imo. Either way I’d sit hours and hours just to enjoy the Spirit of Japan showcase","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC3vuqCmAE&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2024-11-6"},{"Name":"Doron Privé","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKkdtQAXsQ4Kx4kWjv6_tOt_smbLwFWiIFkEVxSgS1lbwIt1iE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I went there with two friends and it was very disappointing. The tour last maximum 10min and was very expansive. If you are not a Spanish speaker, it’s just a waste of money because they are projecting a kind of movie during the second part of the tour and the it’s 100% in Spanish.
The museum is a first corridor with lights and mirror on 10 meters (it’s the  only cool thing you will see), after that there’s the Spanish movie projected on walls, ground and ceiling and then it’s finished. I told you, it last 10 minutes and cost ~20€/person.","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"Szabó Niki","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV84fSuMdL2CvUMYb8KTIx8o8N_acVCSyv6Jhzs_qi8PGzXEFft=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Too expensive for what it is. There’s only 2 room, and we thought there will be more to see. There was only a few people inside and wasn’t any line at the entrance.
I really liked the horror part though, that was pretty cool.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB_ebNOiQ5DETbdlRgz2VtP&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2025-10-22"},{"Name":"Elona Mendelovich","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXAv8jvh5kGg_s9ACrPeqXHq8uDb5qWUTkl7ZQw69QCYwrK5-qNUw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The place is well-designed and nice with unique themes. A tourist has a little difficulty understanding some of the displays or characters, but I'm sure locals understand everything. Horror movie lovers have an impressive portion of the display.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDEnRRUHgJ1T6epDxqx-1OE&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2025-10-16"},{"Name":"Ray Recce","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVaLUzLvo5cjXZS1ZzCMxys31WGA7W0ybQPm3fN8OEkc5vtYjM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We had a nice visit with family, we specially liked the horror part. A lot of famous people to see and the train was also a nice experience. We bought the tickets online but there was no queue at the museum.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC7jsjMdA&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2024-8-15"},{"Name":"Mariangela de Gemmis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXdSMyx0obDpTCrQyAZqsYS8HR7RH0Kbw6FCjqH_fUIMVrn6l0OPQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A very nice experience, would recommend. In the ticket are included 3 "galleries" to visit, a small fun horror area upstairs and a "train of terror" ride, all inside of that building! I was surprised by how big in reality it is, they planned it very well.

The statues are incredibly detailed but some of them feel cramped and out of place? (Like Leonardo Di Caprio's statue in a modern suit in the Western area). But overall I loved the museum!

They also have an AR experience on their website which you can activate through the QR codes! They give additional information about the characters in display and activities that make you temporarely unlock filters for instagram and tiktok, it's very cute. Only flaw is that the website was very slow and because of that it took a lot of time for me to visit the exposition... almost 3 hrs! But you will take way less if you speed up or ignore the QR codes I guess. Your choice!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDbr93UBQ&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2024-8-10"},{"Name":"Vivo Vázquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLvUyRyXlZBRb-TT4BhrnwZ8T78FNkcDjPnfNdsrNdFZg9teg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo de Cera in Madrid is a fun and entertaining place for people of all ages. The museum showcases a wide variety of wax figures, including both Spanish and international celebrities, famous athletes, and historical personalities. The dedicated horror section adds a unique touch of excitement. Staff are helpful, and the experience is well organized, with easy access and no long queues. If you enjoy exploring cultural attractions or taking creative photos, this museum is an enjoyable stop during your visit to Madrid.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Cherry Blossom","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVzTc_9PiI119Xy0q0bT8F1EkxZ5bBoeD_190WYbA3Wn9pk_BJ_=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Only some of the attendants are helpful, the others would just stare at you instead of showing you the way to go (not marked otherwise).
The museum has got a majority of Spanish personalities/ celebrities. It's got a dedicated horror upstairs, although it all feels a bit creepy. 😄","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chryso Konstantinou","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVak3LGZKu-DY7RPzeR_-fFfSuk4zZ5ICrZySNLg5dSR4y4d1cZ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"We visited the Wax Museum and were very disappointed with the top floor experience. It is supposed to be entertaining and fun, not something that scares visitors away. The staff member upstairs yesterday really frightened us — he chased us almost to the very end. I was with my 7-year-old son, and in the end we had to turn back and leave because we were both so terrified that it was no longer enjoyable.

There should be limits to this kind of performance. It’s fine to create a spooky atmosphere, but not to pursue visitors all the way through. That only ruins the experience and achieves the opposite of what it should.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Johnny Rodriguez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWiul4NlavDOHRZ5rs97Ih05Ja0sL6bIWBFTlxJECvl0fKzmRGx=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"During my recent visit to the wax museum in Madrid, I had the pleasure of being guided by Yini, an outstanding tour guide who truly enhanced my experience. Yini's knowledge and enthusiasm brought each wax figure to life, making the tour both educational and entertaining. Her engaging storytelling and passion for the museum's exhibits made the visit truly memorable. I highly recommend taking a tour with Yini for a fantastic and insightful journey through the world of wax figures. Thank you, Yini, for your wonderful guidance and for making my visit to the museum so special!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICr_YfezAE&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2024-7-10"}]
