[{"Name":"Raz Maoz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU_JjPTGQF7fDVY4j9TjcAXUbfEf0FTyO37LPVvC3DZ87QKsidCeA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We visited this museum with different expectations and both left absolutely fascinated. For my wife, who has studied the history of fashion and haute couture, this place fit like a glove. She found a professional and deep analysis of silhouettes, textiles, and craftsmanship at the highest level.
But the real surprise was for me. As a history buff, I found a comprehensive and gripping chronological journey. The clothes are just the medium: through them, you see the Industrial Revolution, the wars, the political shifts in Spain, and the social transformations of Europe.

It’s far more than a 'costume exhibit' – it’s tangible history, phenomenally organized, from 18th-century corsets to the groundbreaking designs of Balenciaga.
A true hidden gem in Madrid, highly recommended even if you don't consider yourself a 'fashion person'.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lawana “Mitchi” Walker","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKyssC3nkPp_pZqwmcbuNrVmcnOaoycZTYhTvAsDH-Ec2q8UARRw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I enjoyed this museum quite a lot. The descriptions were good for the pieces, and they were arranged in chronological order with historical context for each section. Some of the exhibits are dark -- which is by design. Light is extremely damaging to textiles, and the low light is necessary to preserve the items. The low light of the rooms is to prevent a harsh glare on the glass of the exhibits, making it easier to see the items in the low lighting that is required. The only thing I personally wish was included was more information about the people who made the garments, as well as the processes of making the materials & garments. It was a nice relaxing way to spend a Saturday afternoon.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCwoNnLZA&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2025-3-15"},{"Name":"yael fayna","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJG-0a9FbqzdCVfD7oKkmOCuuvZztmzAR4nBVqvWImted6o3w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It is not at the city center and this is the benefit, very interesting museum I loved the curatorial process in the sexhibition","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBOvLgHqXlu-cq6TWkzZ-q3&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2026-1-27"},{"Name":"Jakub M Ochab","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXWeOsgMckABLzCjQG-D7kanzxb_tm5gLRx84U6XGHKPXlPU4Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We visited the Museo del Traje again over the weekend, and it continues to be one of Madrid’s hidden gems. Despite its location on the edge of the city, it wasn’t very busy—which actually made for a peaceful and enjoyable experience. It’s a perfect option if you’re planning to spend time in nearby parks or looking for a quieter cultural stop away from the city center.

This time, we went specifically for the seasonal exhibitions, and admission was free. The exhibitions were well-curated and offered a fresh perspective on fashion and design. The museum staff were all very friendly and attentive, which added to the overall positive experience.

There’s also a nice café on site where you can relax with some food, coffee, or a drink—ideal for a break during your visit. Don’t miss the gift shop downstairs either. It has a great selection of fashion-related books and unique items that are definitely worth browsing.

All in all, the Museo del Traje is a wonderful blend of fashion, history, and calm atmosphere—definitely worth a visit whether you’re a fashion enthusiast or just looking for a quieter cultural outing in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCeMzq71dIhXAPbZQjIYB1X&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2025-7-20"},{"Name":"Alejandro Perez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKHk2nbyV1HYyUXx8bXnKtGbRG7n3r_5fqwa7ozq4iCVkJWQw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Small and amazing museum. Great place to bring your family!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB8aNK2UHUlAiv9nPBuWhWl&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2025-11-18"},{"Name":"Jelen Sita","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLahMROOG8JNp7RJvZFjIqI8kI5SuUrnqmMJd-MtC6n4zfMvQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The day was one of those frigid windy winter days in Madrid that chill you to the bone, especially if you walk there from the metro station- a good 10 mins away on wide avenues which only act as wind tunnels....When I arrived I was greeted by an impressive über-modern "container- looking" building - the only drawback was the equally impressive set of cement stairs that lead up to the main entrance(*).  I am sure they had a wheelchair accessible elevator from the ground up but I didn't have time nor the inclination to look for it!!
Now as a veritable human popsicle I was greeted, -after trudging up the stairs- by a large but well heated foyer (which was appreciated instantly). It was sweetly staffed by welcoming people who chuckled commiseratingly at my thankful antics.
It is an easy museum to navigate (all on one floor) and the collection here was far fuller (in regards to the male clothing) than the one in Barcelona. It is amazing how, as a society, the man has been pressured from wearing intricate and delicate embroidery with beautiful floral patterns to a stark simple dark clothing with no embellishment excepting, perhaps, the cut. The only nod to a more changeling offer for the guys was the modern and future clothing, which was interesting.
The female showcase was very prevalent throughout the ages and explains pretty well what the intricacy of the layers were. The most amazing is to consider the fact that the apparatus to enhance the "derrier" was ever worn...
Most of the clothing was representative of the high society so I really enjoyed the great collection of what the less "economically proud" wore- which shows far more useful logic in its design.
There is also a modest lineup (13?) of  clothing designed for the blind to be able to touch and get a feeling of the changes in clothing (mostly female, one male) during the ages. I found this wonderfully inclusive. They could offer a taped explanation (or on tablets in Braille?) of each piece (in the "sighted" part) for those that are recently blind but remember colors and textures?
I would have liked a bit more info of each: the materials used, the name of the designer (if known), the price it might have cost (translated to nowadays euros).  One thing that was commendable is that all the explanations, that were there, were translated into English.
I would suggest they look into using  l.e.d. lighting for those windows where light was very dim so not to damage the cloth...what is the use if you can't see it?!

Overall a great museum! Doable in one hour or so (since it has few placards), with a couple of didactic screens to explain some of the displays...

(*)Oh there is also a lovely looking cafe and a temporary collection (they were announcing "Jeans") both of which are at ground level but I didn't have the time to visit.
I highly recommend it (but dress -ha!- well if coming from the metro in winter time!)
P.S. I asked if they ever staged a real "runway"(pasarela) of modern designs (they could be students' creations?!) but they didn't understand my question and seemed eager to close...","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID1gYWo2QE&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2024-1-7"},{"Name":"Seokjin Ham","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIhtf6A7X2UDP--Xi1vhwXxfhLJk0GUh_pTYJvgqrBOTATbUg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"This is one of the national museums where you can explore the history of Spain's traditional clothing and fashion. The building was built in 1973, but the current museum opened in 2004. It houses more than 160,000 items of clothing, accessories, and textiles. Because it is relatively quiet, it is easy to obtain an annual membership to the national museums. There is a beautiful garden nearby, so you can enjoy a leisurely walk.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDP7JTNRQ&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2024-11-29"},{"Name":"Kathy Patton","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUI5xWFJ7mPNsJDqWmP_hMM6aI9XBZmZV1AQIcLLMEYEq0_zx4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Such an interesting museum learning about Spanish history through clothing across the years. We really enjoyed our visit, especially on a hot day in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDrhYapjgE&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2024-7-18"}]

title: Museo Ciencias Naturales
address: Chamartín, 28046 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Maxim BK","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXhjS8BtCwS0iOd3gnZ1TwfD3D2_kseC3i6FY8AXKrHZx2JMIZFUQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Visité el Museo de Ciencias Naturales un fin de semana sin demasiadas expectativas y, en general, la experiencia fue positiva. Es un museo cuidado, con exposiciones claras y bien explicadas, especialmente pensadas para un público familiar. Se nota bastante ese enfoque: muchos paneles didácticos, maquetas grandes y zonas pensadas para niños, lo cual está bien si vas con críos, aunque si buscas algo más profundo o especializado puede quedarse un poco corto. Aun así, hay salas interesantes, como la de biodiversidad y fósiles, donde me entretuve más de lo que esperaba. El edificio es amplio y se recorre con calma, sin sensación de agobio. En mi caso fui entre semana y estaba tranquilo, lo que se agradece. No es un museo que te vuele la cabeza, pero sí un plan agradable para pasar un par de horas aprendiendo algo y desconectar del ruido de la ciudad.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"A O","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXMiIq1Xyp9MqeBs443BWLry63RuqPpQMWYDVUjWQj4PBmqtBND=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Hemos ido al Museo de Ciencias Naturales a ver la exposición de Juan Ramón Martín titulada Kranion, este escultor hace un estudio de los cráneos de diversas especies animales en unas bellas composiciones de hierro. Su obra es impresionante , refleja el alma de las especies con gran sencillez de formas. Os recomiendo que visitéis la exposición que estará hasta finales de Julio.
Contigua hay otra exposición “Dibujando entre leones”, con unos bonitos dibujos de estos felinos .
Aprovechamos para visitar el museo , con piezas de principios del Siglo XX algunas de las cuales podían haberse renovado ya que han perdido el color, si bien es cierto que se pueden contemplar gran variedad de especies de nuestro país","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID47KH4xgE&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2019-5-12"},{"Name":"Fernando R.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW3g4SyvCMQRxYY4RhtzYhZwQ0c-BUdBDreKXSNolhkr2uR6xDt=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Muy recomendable. No es quizá demasiado grande pero tiene una exposición muy representativa y es muy didáctico.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKELWirfenibLRhgE&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2025-6-8"},{"Name":"Angel Lillo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUdLxuJxr1HCkfLs-WOngjoMyZ2MA5-cnUl3XIBXwIrvtzojynBCg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Museo pequeño y bien conservado. Es bastante molesto coincidir con varios grupos de colegios sobre todo cuando son adolescentes en plena edad del pavo. Hay una pequeña zona azul para aparcar a cada lado pero mejor ir en transporte público","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC4usfb_QE&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2018-3-20"},{"Name":"Teresa PGT","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJAdkENhJmyxCNtvBYf5PrBPf3Ywp5vXFRzf_2Qwh5tq6lBZg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"El pintacaras de dinosaurios estuvo bien para acercar el museo a los niños. Sitio interesante","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Dolly Bals","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUaWDFzicOIsNv3JZxewTqNtBAFIqU-FC1QxQE_w8CfbL9AtIM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Fuimos con mucha ilusión al Museo de Ciencias Naturales de Madrid, pero la experiencia fue decepcionante. El museo cierra a las 15:00 y no es imaginable para un turista con niños este horario que no figura en Google, no se puede aprovechar bien la visita si llegas cerca del cierre. Solo pudimos estar 40 minutos.

Además, el museo tiene dos entradas separadas por unos 100 metros, y solo en la más pequeña se pueden sacar entradas, algo confuso si llegas sin saberlo. El personal solo parecía preocupado por que saliéramos cuanto antes. No hubo ni un mínimo de amabilidad o interés en facilitar la visita. Daba la sensación de que estaban deseando terminar su jornada y poco les importaba la experiencia de los visitantes, que hemos pagado una entrada completa como si fuéramos a disfrutar del horario y museo completo.

Una pena, porque el contenido del museo es muy interesante, pero la gestión y el trato al visitante dejan mucho que desear. Deberían revisar sus horarios, su política de entradas y sobre todo, la actitud del personal de cara al público.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"juanjo ovied","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIIyqNL2004eo6S_Bi4f1cyNyPutXmkp11yuhI0xcWRUSAjdw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Parada cercana al Museo de Ciencias Naturales, ETS de ingenieros industriales,que comparten manzana con El Laboratorio de electrotecnia, la Residencia de Estudiantes, el Ramiro de Maeztu, y Educación de la CAM,  también està Nuevos Ministerios, iglesia de San Juan de la Cruz.¡Ahh! Se me olvidaba, el monumento del pueblo de Madrid a la Constitución, habitualmente con estudiantes sentados.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID40uXIDg&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2017-7-9"},{"Name":"Marta FG","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX-00NmE3AnJC7z8tsktVkxGNg6TXDeLdY7eHvO0NFyjRbpxfxI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Hemos hecho la visita con peques y ha estado genial, les llamaba la atención todo y les ha gustado mucho. Es un museo relativamente pequeño dividido en dos edificios, no se hace pesado de visitar. Muy visual e ilustrativo. Buena actividad para hacer con niños!!!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICcipjs6gE&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2019-12-1"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"vicetravellers","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWqff5QMzzqdnjeGHbPvbc5EDj2433svwF5jcMJTM7qHU5ZLWM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Thyssen-Bornemisza National Museum is an exceptional museum that completes Madrid’s art triangle beautifully. The collection is incredibly well curated, offering a smooth journey through different periods, styles, and artistic movements without feeling overwhelming.

What makes the Thyssen stand out is its balance. From old masters to impressionism and modern works, everything is displayed in a way that feels coherent, calm, and engaging. It is a museum where you can truly take your time and enjoy the art rather than rush through it.

An essential visit in Madrid for anyone interested in painting, culture, and thoughtful curation. Elegant, accessible, and genuinely inspiring.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBdWidapaCnYGeYfb_ZWDy4&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-1-29"},{"Name":"Juliana C M","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXYDWugloZlh79bbKifo16IVCS1CM67BsOBsP__7PPYAZBofac=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Amazing museum that covers almost 700 years of paintings. If you go on Mondays entrance is free from 12:00 till 16:00.  The museum is huge and I would recommend going in different days if you have time. I will add some painting photos :) I used AI to stroll around and experience hand picked paintings and worked really well.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhANYkkmiZmYShE9LLYAzsEs&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-1-12"},{"Name":"KP G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX2Rg9DiQ29ajhUkI-XwqOIh23_chg3Bw9nylChO9JBI-09zvil=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"What a fabulous collection! If you like impressionist and expressionist art, you’d likely enjoy this museum a lot.

Inclusive design, in the sense of having displays for touching, with braille and audio descriptions, for low/no vision visitors. I haven’t seen this attentive detail in any other museum. Bravo to the curators.

Website says Mondays are free. Another great feature of this museum.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAvf1BsJeyyrMDNaQwCaNCt&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-1-21"},{"Name":"Alex Veziro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV_prC4j81QgIKfVHsb1zgnDfGKJ6o0QVeve6gytMISddtq28WetA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Thyssen-Bornemisza Museum offers a very complete and enjoyable experience, both in terms of its collection and its overall atmosphere. The variety of artworks, ranging from classical pieces to modern and contemporary art, keeps the visit engaging without feeling overwhelming. The spaces are calm, well lit and easy to move through, allowing you to take your time and really connect with the works at your own pace.
A major highlight of the museum is the staff, who are genuinely excellent. In contrast to the Prado, the employees here are friendly, approachable and clearly willing to help, and they speak English comfortably, which makes a big difference for international visitors. Whether you need directions, information or simple assistance, the interaction feels relaxed and human rather than rushed or strict.
Another strong advantage is that photography is allowed inside the museum. This adds a lot to the overall experience, as you can capture the artworks that stand out to you and engage with them more personally. Compared to the Prado, where this is not permitted, it makes the visit feel more open and memorable. Overall, the Thyssen-Bornemisza combines quality, comfort and excellent service, making it a museum that feels both impressive and genuinely welcoming.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDSdUxpp7tdukVt8c0Iwv9P&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2025-12-27"},{"Name":"L BM","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVkO6B8IbOnTAt2fcyX0LrY9llf-tZHiPSEaVTMZn9T1FP_WwnS=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Beautiful collection! Had a wonderful time admiring the incredible artwork.
An extremely rude tour guide for the museum decided to stand in front of a Caravaggio for nearly 10 minutes as the room filled up with people waiting to see it. She wouldn't move, kept yapping. Even the people in her group realized they were in the way. Eventually someone gently approached her & asked if she could please move aside so that all the rest of the people waiting could view the popular Caravaggio. Her response was that she was with the museum & "there are 800 other works of art you can go look at". I don't care who you work for or how much art there is in the museum, the art is for all of us. Don't be rude.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAqp28PB5JQd5JghpHNSNju&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2025-11-17"},{"Name":"Dana Kay","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVRATWmxn5F6aCV3p5nFeIAGUrPqu7BA-nlAp1x2cUG0B9GB8Bl=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The museum offers an impressive art collection spanning from the early Renaissance to contemporary works. It’s divided into two sections: the permanent exhibition on the first and second floors, and a loaned collection on the ground floor. The galleries are organized by artistic style, so similar works are displayed together, making it easy to appreciate the evolution of each movement. During my visit, I was fortunate to catch two exceptional pairings of exhibitions—Picasso with Klee, and Andy Warhol with Jackson Pollock. It was a rare treat to see these artists presented side by side.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD1xB8M03zFpuGHASrpQF1V&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2025-12-27"},{"Name":"In the pocket Drum studio","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUIXESsegfBQB9BUlhcmYM2BYMAjmIaoxM6W7gpgRocovBrsDw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"totally more than i've expected!

really impressed by the collections..
stayed 3 hours for whole exibitions💕👍🏼","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBmL4cz-ROreUe_82K3eIJh&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-2-16"},{"Name":"IAN Zhang","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUy_zjCVruENW4GrUB8GggcDbeLevjim5b_QLMkM69oxp_5FS_h=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One ticket costs €14. This site offers a historical view of Madrid's water distribution and collection and the evolution of the city. It is free to metro users and offers guided tours.
Although rather small, due to bad management by historical institutions in the past that allowed the destruction of a big part after the discovery, the explanation and guided tour from the team in charge is outstanding.
Free entrance (Just need to pay the regular subway entrance to access the location), but you need to book a visit in the museum web page","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Melinda Merola","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVFngu06ulrW8u0ebmP25yp3ZtA_2hDyVGjpV0tNJZcO5Eqrw4O=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I wasn't sure what to expect from this museum that I'd passed so many times in the Opera metro station. It is a timed entry as this is somehow comparable to the Prado lol","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCbuyqm336NkHDpOCGaREyB&fid=0x0:0x647d32fb7c26d0c3"],"When":"2025-12-22"},{"Name":"Forever 21","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXeydQtCkbieSH3Jfg5kGr9DHxgf2cfwEIwZJGMcFzg0Baayj_cEA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Ignored at reception until I proceeded to enter the tour on my own, denied the first time because my reservation was for a later time so returned later - was told an English tour was no issue but when I returned later I needed to wait for an English guide eventhough supposedly it had been prearranged weeks prior when my booking was made. The museum itself is very small with maybe 20 exhibits targeted to children.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD1KzamxfmVsl9O0K533_bg&fid=0x0:0x647d32fb7c26d0c3"],"When":"2025-11-27"},{"Name":"Amera Althobaity","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL-WSzW1-yKo4-t9z4wElyCejKqyAdPTJpiklMp69GSMYSq_g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Museum of Light (Museo de la Luz) – Madrid

🙋‍♀️ Guide: Very polite, respectful, and one of the best aspects of the visit.

💶 Ticket Price (approx.):

Ester is a super guide and she knows a lot of things. Arts change frequently (it's a private collection) from bizant art to contemporary","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alexander Lipovetskiy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWAXHD6NVrwuD-Q8_6OuD3zViGIEb3NYE_tST12dAhLGUYzcwbo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I came to see works of Salvador Dali as it was advertised online, but found nothing. A stuff member confirmed there is no such at this time. That made me very sad.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"María del Mar Fernandez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX-uXJ0rxzjyDAumGDWr7TxBqGeXc_jRWk6wHxBWYeWgfANeFo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"La visita a las exposiciones temporales ofrece una experiencia correcta. La selección de temas es variada, abarcando diferentes periodos y artistas, lo que permite tener una visión general de distintas corrientes.

La organización de las salas es funcional y el recorrido está estructurado de una manera lógica. Sin embargo, en ocasiones, la cantidad de visitantes puede dificultar la observación detallada de las obras, especialmente en las muestras más populares, por lo que se recomienda comprar la entrada con antelación para asegurar el acceso en la hora deseada.

La información que acompaña a las obras es adecuada, aunque a veces podría ser más extensa para proporcionar un contexto más profundo. En general, es una opción a considerar para complementar la visita a la colección permanente.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marga Corchero","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVlWyxEwOZiFHq03iR3sWkk2iGO95iBcTWB8VMHtbu7Y-PoexzTYg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Maravillosa exposición de Gabrielle Münter. El museo no solo vale por su gran colección de arte sino también por las exposiciones temporales.
Esta concretamente merece mucho la pena y está hasta febrero","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID_mfqENw&fid=0x0:0x9e3319c847b19fdc"],"When":"2025-1-25"},{"Name":"Sara Sáez (Viña)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVLaQkzvq_KUP1rQ3jxoAhpwmnL9tbsunfSGD6oxVueQTHg0b61=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Una exposición increíble que aún puedes disfrutar. "La máquina Magritte” aún estará en este espacio hasta el 30 de enero. El Thyssen es un museo muy especial para mí. Disfruto con la selección de exposiciones temporales que realizan. Esta de Magritte ha sido la última, deseando conocer las próximas obras que se podrán disfrutar.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDmhM61mgE&fid=0x0:0x9e3319c847b19fdc"],"When":"2022-1-18"},{"Name":"Jimena Amor","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW0t77PfCMG8QPjhOf62EKoGK6omfUh4mdhLCAozQjqQXqt7DOd=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Nosotras visitamos Maestras. Fue arte en toda su esencia, dieron voz a artistas femeninas que también revolucionaron y aportaron a la historia del arte. La obra expuesta, fantástica y muy bien seleccionada desde mi punto de vista (aparecen artistas como Artemisia Gentileschi, Maruja Mallo, Tamara Lempicka, Frida Kahlo...).","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDt5PqqSQ&fid=0x0:0x9e3319c847b19fdc"],"When":"2024-2-2"},{"Name":"fernandonajera19","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU18Uk_JwcFouWUrDWav4h9vFfKY6pCasSNfrbMM2eJucy2joKr=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"La exposicion "la Reina Roja de Palenque" es simplemente un timo. Te hacen pagar la entrada al museo para luego ver unos pocos objetos en una habitación del tamaño de mi salón. Se supone que estas acciones van a salvar la maltrecha relación politica con Mexico.... No se yo....","Images":null,"When":""}]

Though primarily focused on the “Blue Division” the museum also covers and showcases Soviet forces in World War II and briefly on the Spanish Civil War.

Highly recommend for any military history buff.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Spencer N","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIhrhPaALvuvmE97cK5y8cNv0RlnijgWlE_9iPLD-UWV9mUYMA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"It is very inconvenient to visit this museum. Ho contattato i curatori i quali mi hanno detto che la causa è l’intervento della “autorità “ (il governo?). Si pregano quindi i curatori del museo di apportare la dicitura “chiuso” sul sito Google anziché “aperto il mercoledì “","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDopZKEQExBPGJRwjwLyG8N&fid=0x0:0x26395748a589a3e5"],"When":"2025-11-19"},{"Name":"Jose Andrés Elizondo Fdez-Cuervo /Eli","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVTzSJ45JY2HxmeixiDtuHFMsl7-60fJKPVbBsK__jj-keyD7mtcQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Una pasada y un pasado oculto que conviene recordar.
Es impresionante la de objetos auténticos que tienen.
Y si tienes la suerte de una visita guiada es genial.
Es pura historia sin polarizaciones de un pasado que forma parte de nuestra historia de España 👌💯👍","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Nuan Masa Eros","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLqeUSl3WORL5a3CMEboXX3uWCC__p1bBDzJqnXL5DMr8s_kg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Este museo dedicado a la historia de la División Azul es un lugar único en Madrid. Ofrece una visión histórica detallada sobre este episodio de la historia española. Las exposiciones son muy completas, con uniformes, documentos y objetos personales de los soldados. Es un espacio educativo que permite entender mejor este capítulo histórico. La visita resulta muy interesante para los amantes de la historia militar. Muy bien conservado y organizado.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Gus Tavo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWuxoqR3ojExsmsTr9ZJQVldKB5wCwjEjMFC1oZPQktN0nCgUx9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Tenía muchas ganas de visitarlo y al final lo he conseguido gracias a la invitación de uno de sus miembros, pues no es un museo al uso. La colección de material es increíble, las maquetas Y recreaciones muy buenas, y el guía explica muy bien la historia de esos españoles que fueron a luchar a Rusia. Llamad y concertar la visita antes de ir o no podréis entrar.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDJ3ImcUg&fid=0x0:0x26395748a589a3e5"],"When":"2023-7-12"},{"Name":"Ciber Hispania (Edu)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVMNOqbr6iKDl6ErhbaIOJQrZPmY52HQXHv6syBmZIa2h6yXGku=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Increíble la acumulación de objetos militares originales.
Muy meritorio el Trabajo de Recreación de zonas del Frente, o el hospital, o la Cantina.
Excelente colección de uniformes, medallas.
Excelente explicación por los voluntarios de la Asociación.
Zona de Bar a precios muy bajos.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC44s7CKQ&fid=0x0:0x26395748a589a3e5"],"When":"2017-6-9"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"PL Lau","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX_kjCx3N7Oe5EIg03qgHTSqYbHJ9Dr8h6_hZbfJqZYWeYejkv9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A Nostalgic Trip Through Gaming History
⭐⭐⭐⭐

If you have any interest in gaming, OXO Museum is an absolute must-visit in Madrid. It turned into an amazing walk down memory lane for us!

What we loved:

Hands-on Experience: The biggest plus is that you can actually play almost everything. We saw everything from old Commodore computers and classic Sega machines to Pac-Man and a massive range of retro and modern systems.

Childhood Memories: We spent a fantastic couple of hours playing on my childhood console. It was the perfect way to relive old memories while making new ones.

Educational Context: It’s not just an arcade; there are great historical explanations, descriptions, and exhibits that really give you a sense of the evolution of the industry.

A few things to note:
Price: At €21, it is a tad pricey compared to other museums in the city.

Time Limit: Your visit is limited to two hours, which felt somewhat restrictive. Given how much there is to see and—more importantly—play, you have to keep an eye on the clock!

Overall: Despite the price and time limit, it’s a fantastic, well-curated space. Perfect for families or anyone looking to reconnect with their favorite classic games.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBkStWpxMgrPoRNvWDgtfnw&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-2-8"},{"Name":"Diego Schwarz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIqpGpnlnPKQWw4-vgrZrUxVnsOcgERKIZ7Z9T8_P5T6tSZqA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Honestly, we weren’t expecting much — and wow, were we wrong! 😄 It turned into an amazing walk down memory lane. We had a fantastic couple of hours playing on my childhood console, reliving old memories and making new ones at the same time. Such a fun, special experience for both my kid and me. Highly recommended!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAedZjQDHFVCjbXap6SINXI&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-1-4"},{"Name":"Joe Szabo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWqlMOLx09MrD42Yu00kBfRybp1LlyD_EY8OUGGMpNkMAlhnys4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"OXO Museo del Videojuego Madrid is a fantastic place for anyone interested in the history of video games. Spread across three floors, the museum showcases every generation of gaming from the 1960s and 70s all the way to the latest PlayStation consoles.

You can try almost everything — from old Commodore computers and classic Sega Machines and Pac-Man games to a wide range of retro and modern systems. There are historical explanations, descriptions, exhibits, and plenty of hands-on gameplay, which is a big plus.

The adult ticket is €21, which I find a bit expensive, and the visit is limited to two hours, which feels somewhat restrictive considering how much there is to see and play.

Overall, I’d give it 4 stars — but for anyone who loves video games, it’s definitely worth a visit.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDMjCWyiWGSXjNSHlW9acs-&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-11-18"},{"Name":"Siv Carina Molvik","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWfHfJ-c2ljwqULxk8LJK81CWRBkqoj4OUL8Uduvdie9XnbnCI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Whether you are a younger gamer who loves dropping Tilted Towers and playing LEGO versions of games or an older gamer who remembers PONG fondly like it was yesterday, this place is going to amaze you! Spend a couple of hours walking down memory lane of games and play ALL of them! Interactive museum indeed! Highly recommend!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBCro9zn2JwMcS7TJhlB_dG&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-1-26"},{"Name":"Kam Loqueman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUxC4clvyzC3x-fNmSBYU0Vrhvk05LFQnTDSuN-p7D6-yD4FCzj-Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One could argue a tad pricey at 21 Euros but for me well worth it. Brought back so much nostalgia and the fact you could touch and play with 40+ year old tech was brilliant. The arcade was great fun playing games of my youth..for free! The Lego exhibition was a bonus.

I visited on a Saturday and it wasn't too busy at all.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBSR2u9NhpzjJrowg7093ry&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-11-16"},{"Name":"Vivian Martinez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVxe-YsjP5k6BiGnY8-7zA7gI6OHs8j_MTCaODEYXkYCZxE__0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"If you’re a video game fan, this one’s for you! Have never seen anything like it. 7@ years worth of video game history, you get to play in all (and I mean ALL) the consoles you can think of from 70 years back. Truly a blast from the past! Make sure to make a reservation.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAsPWVPbdWKhFNzLTKmrwIA&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-10-7"},{"Name":"Lawana “Mitchi” Walker","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKyssC3nkPp_pZqwmcbuNrVmcnOaoycZTYhTvAsDH-Ec2q8UARRw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Went on a weekday afternoon and there were only a couple other families there.

It is a little expensive BUT they do have a reduced rate for families, & an annual pass that I personally think is worth it. I've seen some reviews that said some of the games didn't work, but I only saw maybe 3 or 4 games/systems that weren't working. The history of videogames part is super cool, & they have a huge selection of gaming systems on display & available to play. The arcade is also super cool, the Japanese games were the most fun for my son. The PlayStation floor was pretty cool, too. The entire place is themed very well, it's clean, in good condition & a nice place to spend a couple of hours as a family.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBmtrAubrN2mFcA5v0O8vhQ&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-7-3"},{"Name":"Diogo Morais","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLLo3-eNubWf7buJzmV4e2jyPV9FE1UJME0WrCQxukl-eqvWQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Very good museum with a lot of games and arcades to try out!
My biggest critic is that the parents don't understand their kids can't be on the same game for long time... Needs more control on that part a lot of machines had the same kids there for more than 10 minutes....","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAshlO6DoTDkUTtdUNcLoLA&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-9-13"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Michael Offord","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUCYcnQ9p_fF7bpz5p0dlxJtJ-6yRZkgizWzoZieS9pO2nu0Dw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a really beautiful place with an interesting family History. The Dukes of Alba have a long history in Spain and are still close to the monarchy. However, they are also Dukes of Berwick in Scotland, and claim descent from the Stuarts. There are many paintings of Stuart ancestors, including Henry VII, Mary Queen of Scots, Charles II and James II. The family moved to France during the Glorious revolution and served the French Monarchy before moving to Spain.

The state rooms are luxuriously decorated and the gardens are very beautifully presented.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBFiQ1B0fRXRTDr6zsZRScp&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2026-2-21"},{"Name":"Lucas Kovács","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUAilLNzd-8YrLSM-6EX9NLiSIkwgxKq6129nEafcsyV9U-HPsV=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Best advice I can give you! Visit on Tuesday afternoon or Sunday afternoon — tickets are only €10 instead of the regular €22. Also, don’t miss the stables! Most people rush straight into the palace and completely skip them, but the visit starts there. Bonus tip: there are free tickets booking online on Mondays at 10AM. They are available one week in advance.

The palace was quite impressive, beautifully maintained and rich in history. It houses more than half of Christopher Columbus’ autograph documents — meaning original handwritten documents by Columbus himself, which is pretty remarkable.

Inside, it’s not just about paintings (though there are many, with references to several famous painters). You’ll also find original decorations, furniture, sculptures, everyday objects, and a large number of family portraits spanning generations, which really helps tell the story of the place over time.

I visited on a Tuesday afternoon, and it was not too crowded, making it easy to enjoy everything at a relaxed pace. The palace is closed on Mondays afternoon.

An audio guide is available in English, Spanish, French, and Portuguese. There are 14 rooms plus the gardens included in the visit, which didn’t take more than an hour.

A note on the gardens: the French gardens were not fully open during my visit. They’re currently only visible from a balcony near the front entrance on the left side, which is a bit of a shame but still nice to see from above.

Practical details: lockers, a shop, and toilets are available near the stables. There’s no café on-site.

Overall, a rich, well-curated palace visit with excellent explanations and great value — especially if you catch the reduced-price slots. Highly recommended.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCsvYw5fRn8xWi9Xi7MOJRZ&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2026-2-3"},{"Name":"David Le Hen","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIs6VMqQkeI5f0KchTSIRDqNEm803FMAwTTPKCaA4J-DKI9vQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Beautiful palace from the Alba family. Must see if you  go to Madrid. Fascinating family.
Very helpful staff. The rooms are very well preserved .
It was very quiet today which made it even better experience.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAsMWwwmt0QwDbS8KbJK5wc&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2026-1-12"},{"Name":"Francisco Mortágua","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX7sxvZJCTDiKD-D3AFqbg2AnCG54qTFAk8k5PUqPEL1D9i3GBS=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"A nice palace with some interesting objects, paintings, and information about the Alba dukedom and family. However, while it’s understandable that funding is needed for its preservation, the visit doesn’t quite justify the €22 ticket per person. Not really recommended — there are much better options to visit in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB1g8T3lUiorQuy-oOSntbe&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2025-10-26"},{"Name":"Michelangelo Wegner","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIKHfAfojH9PkxUzgYpV7xFhOXOpHV47i2KwQoGgddGKIM1ew=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Liria Palace is a neoclassical palace. It is the Madrid residence of the Dukes of Alba. A beautiful building and grounds. Allow time to explore the interior for the beautiful interior decorations and artwork.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD9N2WeHIvTWx2OrxM7n-hB&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2026-1-19"},{"Name":"Joanna M L Fearn","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUOfKWToaG8brnoJqigVBfiG4fJXSTatoZ79fuBMUEzCGoYUZ4E=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I specifically came to Madrid, to see the artist Joana Vasconcelos exhibition at the Palacio de Liria as I admire her work and thought the exhibition looks amazing.

This was my first time in Madrid. The Palace isn't hard to find, I walked there from Temple of Debod, it was about 10 mins. You can't see anything from the entrance because of the trees, the entrance is small, you show your ticket then go through airport style security then access to the garden to the palace.

There is a small prefab style building to the left which has lockers, toilets and a small gift shop.

The palace is very impressive. I had a wonderful time, there is so much to see and look at. It is very ornate and rich in detail. You are allowed to take photos as long as you don't use flash photography.

The gardens weren't open, not sure why, you can only access front of the palace, not the back. Nevertheless it didn't detract from my visit. The exhibition was well put together, I could take in everything at my own pace without being rushed and it wasn't overcrowded.

Joana Vasconcelos work is fascinating and intruiging, from stiletto shoes made from stainless steel pans and lids to Crochet covered dogs and abstract ornate figures. There is detail everywhere you look.

This was definitely worth the short trip to Madrid from London. I had a wonderful time. (July, 2025)","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAPaa-z6oy_cyiRjC-HHR0G&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2025-8-16"},{"Name":"joanna esparza","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXsdRcH_GPPGNKN6XfLgaY1Z5zL7aEqkCPHvXTlk1pq3AsuzBjQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I went as soon as they opened. That helped me not feel rushed to go thru the whole building and it was quiet inside. I recommend earbuds so you can listen to the audio guide they have available in English. It comes with a lot of great info for every room.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCEMVwqJkt9-LjBtfdPXdd6&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2025-9-28"},{"Name":"Jon Flynn","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXg5z6yaTbyt8STy1CXUcErg67hyrjKhM-1tFse9DOxsX4A5jaV=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The palace is stunning and with the current exhibition you are also allowed to take photos. BUT for 23 euros you are NOT allowed in the garden. This is unacceptable. nevermind my life was I refused to walk around the gardens, they are far more interesting than the odd exhibition they have at the moment. Therefore only 4 stars. The staff were charming.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD75eb5UyMBCwO3rwbyWB9Z&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2025-6-23"}]

title: Museo Sorolla
address: Chamberí, 28010 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Jose Garcia","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVZ3giLPz2ziBWCFCbpRI3YaT88dz_l2MtIJzKAAnNPitSP6oCwaA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This beautiful place is run with an absolute ignorance to the customer needing special assistance.
When we asked if wheelchair accessible they excused this necessity by "It is stated in our website we don't "
The elderly and immobile have to walk long distances climb steps and are barred from the second floor.
If you are a costumer needing special assistance this museum discriminates against you.
On top of that the staff is rude to begin with.
Be careful  Museo Sorolla the world is reading this in this part of the web.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Nuan Masa Eros","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLqeUSl3WORL5a3CMEboXX3uWCC__p1bBDzJqnXL5DMr8s_kg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El Museo Sorolla es un tesoro escondido en el barrio de Chamberí que conserva la esencia del gran pintor valenciano Joaquín Sorolla. Este museo-casa permite conocer tanto la vida como la obra del artista en un entorno íntimo y auténtico. El edificio mantiene la distribución original con el taller del pintor, sus salones y un precioso jardín andaluz diseñado por el propio Sorolla, lleno de fuentes, azulejos y vegetación mediterránea. La colección permanente incluye numerosos óleos que muestran su dominio magistral de la luz y el color, especialmente en sus famosas escenas de playa. El museo no está masificado, lo que permite disfrutar de las obras con tranquilidad. La entrada tiene un precio muy accesible. Es una visita imprescindible para los amantes del impresionismo español y el arte decimonónico.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Francisco Javier Toribio Dominguez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIQOaCV23hOJZyXCwvLI2zjGRUGwGI6p2RNfRO2lHV6TycI8A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Pequeño, pero gran museo con gran multitud de obras del genial artista, y en estos momentos y por poco tiempo mas con una colección de trajes y complementos repartidos entre este museo y el thyssen muy interesantes y con un patio en pleno centro de Madrid que nos lleva a tiempos pasados.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE7Y-bEg&fid=0x0:0x493b2c9b15e9fd5e"],"When":"2018-5-21"},{"Name":"Ulricus Angelus","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW-TXBol6ahJS4nIPaNJPPjeTbb1l9alGTE43IXPPHSeqc8qPvS=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wunderschönes Museum in eines kleinen schattigen Park umgeben von vielen höheren Häusern. Wohnhaus, Arbeitsplatz und Galerie des großen Malers. user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Frankie Said","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU2J5NW-errqnDhcVVx-9IjO6uelZ4NwhNAbh1cFyVEiXqfjgpD=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Amazing showcase of old metro trains, it is also free to visit with a valid ticket!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBPiPoi6YXXr5eWe_wYS2Sk&fid=0x0:0xa06a42c523ec7c7d"],"When":"2025-12-9"},{"Name":"M Jones","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVmJwIo0JukAFUEW1bYnsG_mzWp9ZqEKaH4p1YsaoAOTVtp40yZ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A fascinating glimpse of the past with the original Metro carriages arranged for a viewing, though it’s a shame that you can’t go into at least one of them, to feel the atmosphere. Behind the carriages are items such as doorhandles to switching gear, arranged in viewing cases.
TIP: if arriving by the Metro, don’t leave by the barriers to go to the upper concourse, because the Metro museum is at the level of these barriers on the left-hand side. If you go up to the top concourse, as we did, very small signs point back the way you came and we spent 20 minutes going back-and-forth until we finally found somebody to take us back through the barriers and point to the entrance of the museum. It really is very poorly signposted.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDFyIX5hwE&fid=0x0:0xa06a42c523ec7c7d"],"When":"2023-11-4"},{"Name":"Tomas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWG4N_xEANm6FTsCjmbhpXPZnJVFWLrRkd_JtGvw59x8QrXquQn=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Great place, where you can see some old metro 🚇 vehicles in a very good shape.
Also in this exhibition it is possible to get information about the history of the Madrid metro 🚇.
And all of that without paying any entrance fee.
Sadly, you can't go inside these vehicles 😔.
There are staff on site, who can answer any questions if needed.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDF4_evAw&fid=0x0:0xa06a42c523ec7c7d"],"When":"2023-11-9"},{"Name":"Manuel Gonzalez Vazquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW7DEtHZo8-9cH9OphhBsTFwF_pw91tcJyp1d4OGjmYWJ_mo1KK2w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The Museo Metro Chamartín is a space where you can discover the history of the Madrid Metro through an exhibition of classic trains and informative panels. It's one of the five historical spaces of the metro system and offers a glimpse into the past of this important mode of transport in Madrid
Access: Advance reservation is required for guided tours and recommended for self-guided visits.
Price: Free admission with a transport ticket.
The tour guide was really nice a made the experience both interesting and fun","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tim S","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVir8Qy9hVhBpfrpURplKPzjOC1VCr_-PPL7_fUBNM9OpIruLEOhg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"There's not a great deal here apart from old carriages but it's a good way to kill time while waiting for a train. All written info is Spanish only... As usual, the hours in Google Maps are wrong - but it seems they only open when they feel like it... It's free too.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICRsbreiwE&fid=0x0:0xa06a42c523ec7c7d"],"When":"2023-4-4"},{"Name":"David Antonio","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJXivTlIJSns9xqV01C0nu6oPEAkd2AsJrIRwscYBd94ryIew=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The tickets are free for students under 25 years old and senior citizens. The rooms were incredibly ornate, full of painting and grand reception rooms.
The gift shop was full of great options.
Unfortunately the cafe is closed.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Carlos Guerra","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXhX_SlrrlJrPxHz3M4ATPCqfsOVo-PJG3HC9pfpzkg-croDzDl=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very informative and entertaining. You can learn a lot about the history of the city through the metro. Super recommended :)","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Andie Greene","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJbfW_ZRfncWp-AfhrbrTCeHykyKno56cwYWFYlWD5N6sZH8w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Interesting to see restored train stock.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMDo6Z6hKw&fid=0x0:0xa06a42c523ec7c7d"],"When":"2025-4-26"}]

Very magical. Used to be the cloister of the cathedral and was used in former times for wedding cocktails and such events, now it is still used to host events but within the context of the Prado Miseum","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Carmen mendez ayala","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL-78cNA2vUKujKoiZ2z7MVlQTCbrf4eZalnfSS868UlhzbVA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Esoectacular","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Gogara Arkitektura","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWY6jDT2RpjbdhXi4TeWiLeDVxwVKV-mYRRzgTqNYRnmupeTKwk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El claustro del Museo Nacional Centro de Arte Reina Sofía se percibe como un espacio especialmente agradable dentro del recorrido del museo, pensado casi como una pausa necesaria cuando el cansancio empieza a notarse tras varias salas.

Arquitectónicamente, la solución es muy sugerente. El claustro original aparece suspendido, “volando” sobre una estructura de hormigón visto, que se muestra sin intentar mimetizarse. La estructura no pesa visualmente, sino que enmarca y realza el valor del claustro original.

El patio central actúa como verdadero corazón del espacio. La proporción, la luz y la ausencia de ruido lo convierten en un lugar tranquilo, donde el tiempo parece desacelerarse.

La experiencia de calma la percibimos ya que al ser lunes, el claustro estaba prácticamente vacío, lo que acentuó esa sensación de refugio dentro del museo.

Lejos de ser un espacio residual, funciona como un lugar de descanso real, tanto físico como mental, que equilibra muy bien la intensidad del recorrido expositivo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDik19UFagP_sYEXD5emUej&fid=0x0:0xfd3c86f95e23fa1a"],"When":"2026-2-20"},{"Name":"Francisco Lucena","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXXAnbPEgO38IAgd5mIrYHf4QXKfnMypv1751f6z9U8cL9E_HQHDQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"La ampliación del claustro de los Jerónimos es una espectacular obra de Rafael moneo en 2007 incluyen la recuperación y rehabilitación del claustro de los Jerónimos que perteneció al antiguo monasterio de Jerónimo el real, utilizando gran parte de su estructura original.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAaCF038YEMtiB28iO2eG6_&fid=0x0:0xfd3c86f95e23fa1a"],"When":"2025-9-14"},{"Name":"Fernando Anayet","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUioCJNuamwUKIFiD6_N5CG67YAYQLWgVY6ygBcezFzyMDDJnk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Es parte de la última ampliación del Prado en manos de Moneo.

Es interesante como edificio y alberga las colecciones temporales del Prado, dándole más espacio.

La puerta principal simula la corteza de un árbol.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCYxdCkTg&fid=0x0:0xfd3c86f95e23fa1a"],"When":"2025-5-5"},{"Name":"Etsel Roberto Eschoyez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKCliESF1ORBDFthhaKlTcq5JX4y_bcC2V-L4G98rx9WNp4Iw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Me pareció cara la entrada por lo que se puede ver en su interior. Pensábamos que accederiamos a gran parte del monesterio pero no es así. De lo que se puede visitar, muy lindo.","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Helen Stasa","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXkfbRD5S6JkCLjo4woGkC6XUh2i9JY7Y96xNg9wsDsS9wFsiE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"It’s an interesting idea, but there’s definitely some improvements that could be made.
1) In booking the tour, you’re obliged to have a back-and-forward WhatsApp exchange with an employee about the time. Surely this could be automated? Similarly, the tone of the WhatsApp messages is very patronising- they treat you like you lack basic comprehension skills e.g. “Be advised that the museum is in MADRID.” Yes. We know.
2) The staff member was clearly bored and uninterested and going through the motions. The tour started 10 minutes late (the bossy WhatsApp messages insisted we MUST be early), and, upon noticing that I had an accent, the staff member told me “The tour is in Spanish. It’s not in English.” I told her (in Spanish) that understood that, and that I can understand Spanish. I considered the conversation closed, but at the end of the tour, she again insisted on addressing me in English in front of everyone. I found this impolite, and bad customer service. In addition, she had a bored expression on her face the whole time, which was hardly fun for the kids.
3) The rooms are full of interesting things, but you don’t get given enough time to look at them. Instead, you’re rushed from room to room.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICn09fydA&fid=0x0:0x7180acca61fb61bb"],"When":"2024-9-29"},{"Name":"Carol Matiz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ1TvbhuEQcxzksgRIkjdPZG2rSXSfB66q3q_pi3jXKZDwTMJY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Our visit was great, we love it! The storyteller was excellent just a little fast telling the story, but we still had a very good experience our little one enjoyed it. And they don't let you take pictures or videos.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Amnet Pantaleon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVdOrQg7I6VMdSmP1_34UfqqjOYsmOKaVqCJOzPiGHpUBuQY9oI=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Beautiful place and loving staff.very magical for the little ones and the entire family. Highly recommended.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sarah B","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWJbDlKPDDo4d5sMWGfEHsjEEpymmi8Jw8jqCw_HBuUDm8pw8T_qg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We visited with our three children (ages 10, 9 and 7) after a visit to the Royal Palace.

Initially told the 5pm tour full so we were going to book for 6pm but it became clear we were English so we were allowed to go on our own as likely we don't speak enough Spanish to benefit from guided tour.

The museum is located in a sort of mini shopping arcade on the main road between Sol and Opera. It's easy to miss. We went up to the museum and one of the staff members spoke good English so showed us into the cartoon presentation (in Spanish but understood enough that we got the story) she also gave us a book with the English translation in to read too. We are then given a map (in English) to spot the key items as you move to the three rooms.

It's a very quirky museum full of legend about the tooth mouse and the children enjoyed clambering through the little tunnels and seeing if they started getting an itchy nose. We liked the biscuit tin room and the special post box and the tunnel where they found the special coins left for them.

I would say that at 5 euro per person it didn't feel like amazing value for a family of five BUT that said the children loved it and the member of staff engaged with them beautifully and helped us to find the mouse door out on the main road.

Great little museum if you have children similar ages to ours but not the best value thing you will do during a day in Madrid.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"BasicReviews","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUm35sAVzX2zZyFrASlJrXPsSi8kGFv4Qx_OGrBZHL2J2GI-HxlQA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Awesome place. Nice staff. Good story.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEI7xkqi1_Zz5Bg&fid=0x0:0x7180acca61fb61bb"],"When":"2025-5-23"},{"Name":"Lole Ramirez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLvEBMqQIRYo0vU_HXUyb6ZBsN_Bl8-JeCVOFMX-lOykIJc7g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"It was ok, but not very interactive and our 5 year old did not engage, which is odd as she is every curious.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Monica Lidia Rodriguez Estrada","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVxxKYl6aehcsVQdmZchgXc-dHiaqbW_yDPd0kkbQNCOqZAaDUK=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The staff was nice although the museum was quite small not something I was expecting. I went with my 17 month old and 6 year old.  I was advised by the guide to please step out of the explanation in  case  the baby would start crying, it made sense since it was crowded. and hardly had any room to walk. I would not recommend it for babies unless an adult can stay with them outside.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Cian Donohoe","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUpR7dgPhfhDsUehrwXOBt8f4Z9NWaR-YlOEIb9iyx_rYXfHSAF=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fantastic food and atmosphere. The waiter Juan was incredible, very nice, very funny, and made sure we were enjoying ourselves. Would definitely recommend for an evening meal and some drinks","Images":null,"When":""}]

Distintas exposiciones en unas salas mal aislada y mal definidas.

[{"Name":"Héctor Sedano","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWsOwbcIxSfN5R3erwUEH7988TA-B6DPIEz1n8y5neLGxaWKAQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El nuevo museo está genial. Antes solías estar en el sótano, y daba un aspecto algo abandonado
Ahora mismo se encuentro en una sala muy grande, llena de paneles explicativos y vídeos.
Esta justo antes de entrar a la cafetería
La visita guiada está genial, la guía lo explico todo con mucho detalle y de una forma muy amena.
Torres Quevedo es uno de los grandes inventores del siglo XX, pero que tristemente hoy, es muy poco conocido
En el museo podemos encontrar varios de sus inventos más famosos y hacer un breve recorrido histórico por su vida","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID285m3gQE&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2022-5-24"},{"Name":"Alex Soriano","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVQDetZwOwRcKNg5bR6pvFZNPkpRe4S5ro24rF87uYDZ9oD4e-9=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Museo increíble con piezas únicas. Visita muy buena por parte del profesor Romana. Muchas gracias a todos los partícipes por preservar el patrimonio y legado de este genial ingeniero español.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sagrario Sanchez Francisco","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIEswQ6YBfqYqVyaIIUs_egJhgFmpnTQmJTSbH7j-sYcmDwmg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Un museo y un inventor Cántabro muy desconocido,es el Da Vicci italiano, pero sin fama. Muy interesante y la persona encargada de hacer la visita guiada nos lo hizo muy ameno. Se tarda unos 70/ 80 minutos con guía, si no,en 30 está visto,es pequeño.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"El Mago 2.0","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUm105mBqAlVdvN-fu1L2cpjUw1hE0LiT2Sl_AUt21zwtSE-JUT=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Pregunta por la cafetería de la facultad y verás el museo justo al lado. Suele estar cerrado por lo que para visitarlo hay que concertar una cita mediante email. Totalmente recomendable y sorprendente. Pequeño pero inolvidable museo donde quedarás impresionado por la inteligencia, trabajo y el desarrollo de la carrera profesional del genial inventor Torres Quevedo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC-3Zbl2gE&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2022-11-11"},{"Name":"Castellar zamorano","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVZioBhy_uHgfsIlgCCLt87t8-2HMseMFk7FbRebj8FSXHtB2Ms=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Es un pequeño museo en la Universidad de Caminos en Ciudad Universitaria de Madrid.
Inventor destacado por inventar el Transbordador en las Cataratas del Niágara , actualmente en uso desde 1916.
También los dirigibles, utilizados en la 1a Guerra Mundial, máquina Ajedrecista autómata y máquinas de cálculo y el Telequino, el primer mando a distancia a gran escala, entre otros","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDGtOrv9wE&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2021-11-19"},{"Name":"Javier Pulido Porcel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXlOGHzkxgej9EEi-M_em4uUxnnYYs_jhuv-mBxuduf4FG7QXY=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Buen lugar que visitar si tienes un rato libre, conocer la historia de Leonardo Torres Quevedo un ingeniero con inventos inimaginables, para la época, muy chula la maqueta del teleférico, que es el mismo que  cruza las cataratas del Niágara. Y otros inventos como máquinas de cálculo, dirigibles, mandos a distancia,etc. El guía, que es ingeniero de caminos nos dio una muy buena explicación de cada uno de los inventos presentes y nos hizo el recorrido a través de la historia de Torres Quevedo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICv3u2_xAE&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2024-12-11"},{"Name":"Cristina González","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW4zXb0A_Qr-gplRtewimQsjXPYFCg09HcFz3uvUxizKbuMW42l=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Imprescindible... Se encuentra dentro de la ETSI Caminos, Canales y Puertos en la Ciudad Universitaria.

Es un museo que alberga la colección de máquinas e ingenios diseñados y producidos por el ingeniero Leonardo Torres Quevedo. Se conservan maquetas, aparatos, documentos y fotografías de los inventos.

El gran desconocido para la población y es un español que hizo historia y qué mejor que visitar este museo para poder conocerlo y darlo a conocer. El museo ocupa una sala.

Entrada gratuita y es imprescindible concertar cita previa por correo electrónico. La visita guiada es en días laborales (lunes a viernes) a las 12:00h y de 75-80 minutos de duración. Explicado maravillosamente.

Se puede ir con niños pero recomiendo que a partir de unos 14 años. Para todas las edades en adultos. Grupos de 12 personas para realizar la visita. Al estar dentro de la facultad hay baños y cafetería de los que se puede hacer uso.

Como llegar al Museo Torres Quevedo:
Metro: Ciudad Universitaria (l-6) / EMT: F, G, U, 82, 132

Un poco estrecha la sala pero creo que sí se puede acceder con silla de ruedas sencilla. Es mejor comentarlo al solicitar la cita.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDT7LrBCw&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2024-5-23"},{"Name":"Manuel GD","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXCVXdYApfqmq_XQEcWweualu0tj4DbMma8TLTkZgZveuZWirts=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Acudimos varios alumnos de la Academia de Ajedrez Chamberí y socios del Club de Ajedrez La Didáctica. ¡Una visita estupenda y totalmente recomendable para cualquier aficionado al Ajedrez!
Nos han explicado estupendamente el Museo entero, y muy en particular los 2 autómatas ajedrecistas, primeros de la historia y nos han abierto sus tripas para ver detenidamente el modelo final.
La verdad es que nos dan ganas de repetir.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC2xbXwZw&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2022-4-25"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"PL Lau","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX_kjCx3N7Oe5EIg03qgHTSqYbHJ9Dr8h6_hZbfJqZYWeYejkv9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A Nostalgic Trip Through Gaming History
⭐⭐⭐⭐

If you have any interest in gaming, OXO Museum is an absolute must-visit in Madrid. It turned into an amazing walk down memory lane for us!

What we loved:

Hands-on Experience: The biggest plus is that you can actually play almost everything. We saw everything from old Commodore computers and classic Sega machines to Pac-Man and a massive range of retro and modern systems.

Childhood Memories: We spent a fantastic couple of hours playing on my childhood console. It was the perfect way to relive old memories while making new ones.

Educational Context: It’s not just an arcade; there are great historical explanations, descriptions, and exhibits that really give you a sense of the evolution of the industry.

A few things to note:
Price: At €21, it is a tad pricey compared to other museums in the city.

Time Limit: Your visit is limited to two hours, which felt somewhat restrictive. Given how much there is to see and—more importantly—play, you have to keep an eye on the clock!

Overall: Despite the price and time limit, it’s a fantastic, well-curated space. Perfect for families or anyone looking to reconnect with their favorite classic games.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBkStWpxMgrPoRNvWDgtfnw&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-2-8"},{"Name":"Diego Schwarz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIqpGpnlnPKQWw4-vgrZrUxVnsOcgERKIZ7Z9T8_P5T6tSZqA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Honestly, we weren’t expecting much — and wow, were we wrong! 😄 It turned into an amazing walk down memory lane. We had a fantastic couple of hours playing on my childhood console, reliving old memories and making new ones at the same time. Such a fun, special experience for both my kid and me. Highly recommended!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAedZjQDHFVCjbXap6SINXI&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-1-4"},{"Name":"Joe Szabo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWqlMOLx09MrD42Yu00kBfRybp1LlyD_EY8OUGGMpNkMAlhnys4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"OXO Museo del Videojuego Madrid is a fantastic place for anyone interested in the history of video games. Spread across three floors, the museum showcases every generation of gaming from the 1960s and 70s all the way to the latest PlayStation consoles.

You can try almost everything — from old Commodore computers and classic Sega Machines and Pac-Man games to a wide range of retro and modern systems. There are historical explanations, descriptions, exhibits, and plenty of hands-on gameplay, which is a big plus.

The adult ticket is €21, which I find a bit expensive, and the visit is limited to two hours, which feels somewhat restrictive considering how much there is to see and play.

Overall, I’d give it 4 stars — but for anyone who loves video games, it’s definitely worth a visit.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDMjCWyiWGSXjNSHlW9acs-&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-11-18"},{"Name":"Siv Carina Molvik","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWfHfJ-c2ljwqULxk8LJK81CWRBkqoj4OUL8Uduvdie9XnbnCI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Whether you are a younger gamer who loves dropping Tilted Towers and playing LEGO versions of games or an older gamer who remembers PONG fondly like it was yesterday, this place is going to amaze you! Spend a couple of hours walking down memory lane of games and play ALL of them! Interactive museum indeed! Highly recommend!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBCro9zn2JwMcS7TJhlB_dG&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-1-26"},{"Name":"Kam Loqueman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUxC4clvyzC3x-fNmSBYU0Vrhvk05LFQnTDSuN-p7D6-yD4FCzj-Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One could argue a tad pricey at 21 Euros but for me well worth it. Brought back so much nostalgia and the fact you could touch and play with 40+ year old tech was brilliant. The arcade was great fun playing games of my youth..for free! The Lego exhibition was a bonus.

I visited on a Saturday and it wasn't too busy at all.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBSR2u9NhpzjJrowg7093ry&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-11-16"},{"Name":"Vivian Martinez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVxe-YsjP5k6BiGnY8-7zA7gI6OHs8j_MTCaODEYXkYCZxE__0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"If you’re a video game fan, this one’s for you! Have never seen anything like it. 7@ years worth of video game history, you get to play in all (and I mean ALL) the consoles you can think of from 70 years back. Truly a blast from the past! Make sure to make a reservation.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAsPWVPbdWKhFNzLTKmrwIA&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-10-7"},{"Name":"Lawana “Mitchi” Walker","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKyssC3nkPp_pZqwmcbuNrVmcnOaoycZTYhTvAsDH-Ec2q8UARRw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Went on a weekday afternoon and there were only a couple other families there.

It is a little expensive BUT they do have a reduced rate for families, & an annual pass that I personally think is worth it. I've seen some reviews that said some of the games didn't work, but I only saw maybe 3 or 4 games/systems that weren't working. The history of videogames part is super cool, & they have a huge selection of gaming systems on display & available to play. The arcade is also super cool, the Japanese games were the most fun for my son. The PlayStation floor was pretty cool, too. The entire place is themed very well, it's clean, in good condition & a nice place to spend a couple of hours as a family.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBmtrAubrN2mFcA5v0O8vhQ&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-7-3"},{"Name":"Diogo Morais","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLLo3-eNubWf7buJzmV4e2jyPV9FE1UJME0WrCQxukl-eqvWQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Very good museum with a lot of games and arcades to try out!
My biggest critic is that the parents don't understand their kids can't be on the same game for long time... Needs more control on that part a lot of machines had the same kids there for more than 10 minutes....","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAshlO6DoTDkUTtdUNcLoLA&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-9-13"}]
